job_title: development and implementation of a federated learning system within a Flutter app.
job_description: please bid only if you are ready to do the work within 100 euros. Otherwise please stay away from bidding.  The system's primary objective is to create and refine user profiles based on local data (app usage, browsing history) without transmitting this sensitive information to a central server.  1. User Device Components Local Data Collectors: Modules to collect data from various sources like app usage statistics, browsing history, etc. Data Preprocessing Unit: Processes raw data into a format suitable for analysis. This includes data cleaning, normalization, and feature extraction. Federated Learning Client: This is the core component that runs the federated learning algorithms. It includes: Model Training Module: Trains local models based on user data. Model Update Mechanism: Periodically updates the local model based on new data. Privacy-Preserving Techniques: Implements techniques like differential privacy or data anonymization to ensure user privacy. 2. Flutter App Integration User Interface (UI): Provides visual feedback and interaction options to the user. App Backend: Manages the flow of data between the UI, local data collectors, and the federated learning client. APIs for Data Collection: Interfaces for collecting data from various sources within the device.

job_title: Multimodal Neural Network for Financial Data Analysis and Decision-Making
job_description: Project Overview: I am looking to develop a cutting-edge neural network tailored for comprehensive financial data analysis and informed decision-making. This neural network will be designed to accommodate diverse data types, including video, text, and image formats. Our goal is to create a robust system that can critically assess financial data, propagate errors to enhance accuracy, and make data-driven decisions, ultimately optimizing financial strategies.  Project Scope:  Data Integration: The neural network should seamlessly ingest financial data from various sources, encompassing video content, textual documents, and financial charts in image format.  Multi-Modal Analysis: It should possess the capability to perform multi-modal analysis, extracting meaningful insights from each data type while considering the correlations and patterns that arise when data sources are combined.  Deep Learning Architectures: Employ state-of-the-art deep learning architectures, such as convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for image data, recurrent neural networks (RNNs) for textual data, and spatiotemporal models for video data.  Error Propagation: Implement a robust error propagation mechanism to refine the network's understanding and decision-making ability continually.  Strategic Decision-Making: Develop an intelligent decision-making module that interprets the analyzed data and generates strategic recommendations or actions based on financial goals and objectives.  Training and Validation: Rigorously train and validate the neural network using real financial data to ensure it can handle the complexities and nuances of the financial world effectively.  User Interface: Create a user-friendly interface for end-users, allowing them to interact with the neural network, input financial objectives, and receive actionable insights.  Key Deliverables:  A fully functional neural network capable of analyzing multimodal financial data. A comprehensive error propagation mechanism for continual learning. An intelligent decision-making module offering actionable financial recommendations. Documentation

I am looking for a statistics expert to provide advice on regression analysis. I need assistance in selecting the appropriate procedure for my analysis. I would like to receive the advice within 24 hours.  I'm looking for the best procedure for my case. I have a Pre and Post measurement in Treatment and control group. I have a couple of demographic variables which need to be controlled for.  The catch here is, that I expect a very extreme Mathew Effect to the point where it is possible to see a very low or even negative slope for individuals with low Pre-measurement (baseline) and a positive slope for individuals with a high baseline. So the average in Post-measurement (outcome) in the treatment group may even remain the same as in the control. Because some individuals go up, others go down, depending on their baseline.  The groups are relatively similar at baseline. There is age related change in the outcome measurement to be expected in both groups I'm interested in the change between Pre and Post. Is it bigger in the treatment group? However, given that the baseline measurement may influence this I'm also interested if there is an interaction between baseline and treatment.  Normally without control variables I'd do an ANOVA to look for differences in the mean change between baseline and outcome. But I don't see that capturing my complexities. I could also do an ANCOVA, but I believe this is rather to look for a difference in outcome means adjusted for baselines.  I could also do a regression on the outcome and add an interaction Condition*Baseline to account for the baseline. I'm not sure what speaks against this.  Or I could do some sort of Multi Level Regression. Given my expectation this would need to be a random coefficient model.  Here I could do either a model with, analyzing the trends for each individual. Level 1 - Outcome, PrePost (Time 1 or Time 2) Level 2 - (condition 0/1), Age, Sex, Income, ID (identifies an individual uniquely)  analyzing the trends for each individual.  Or I could do a model with Level 1 - Outcome Level 2 - (condition 0/1), Age, Sex, Income, baseline  Or I could, supposedly do something called a Generalized Estimating Equations (GEE) model, which I just read about today.  So, this is what I came up with, given my training and the ressources I found on the internet and in my books. The normal regression with Condition*Baseline is the one I understand intuitively, but I'm not sure if it is correct for me to use it.  The MLR to analyze the trends seems to be a good thing to use, but as a random coefficient model it only runs with nmle not lme4 without error in R with my data. (too many slopes, error)  I'm looking for advice in justifying to use one method or the other...

The project aims to construct a web-based platform that enables specialized statistical analyses on research data, with an initial focus on Multi-Criteria Decision Making (MCDM) and Structural Equation Modeling (SEM). The architecture will be modular to allow for scalability and the addition of new features and tests in the future.  2. Introduction Background Statistical analyses are crucial in research across various domains. However, the lack of a centralized, user-friendly platform for conducting these analyses has been a significant gap in the research community. This project aims to fill this gap by providing a comprehensive, scalable, and secure web-based platform.  Objectives To develop a web-based platform for statistical analyses To integrate existing open-source Python packages for SEM and MCDM To ensure scalability and extensibility  3. Scope of Work 3.1 Functional Requirements User Authentication and Authorization Users can register using an email address. Email verification for new accounts. Secure login and logout functionality. Role-based access control to differentiate between administrative and regular user capabilities. Dashboard  Users can view a summary of their past analyses. Access to saved data sets and configurations. Profile management options, including password change and account deletion. Data Upload and Management  Support for uploading data in CSV, Excel, and SPSS formats. Data validation to check for missing values, outliers, and format inconsistencies. Metadata management to store additional information about the data sets. Statistical Analysis  Integration of open-source Python packages for SEM and MCDM. MCDM Analysis SEM Analysis  Result Presentation Results will be presented in graphical formats such as scatter plots and path diagrams. Option to download reports in PDF and Word formats. Subscription Plans  A free tier with limited functionalities. Premium tiers with advanced features and priority support. Audit and Logs  Comprehensive logs of user activities for auditing purposes. Error logs to assist in debugging and performance optimization.  Performance The system will be optimized for low latency and high availability. Scalability The architecture will be modular to facilitate the addition of new features and tests. Usability The UI/UX will be designed to be intuitive and user-friendly.  Timeline : One Month

job_title: Appsheet App development as ERP solution for foundry
job_description: **Project Title: AppSheet development for Application for Integrated Production and Management (AppFoundry)**  **Project Description:**  **1. Introduction:** The Application for Integrated Production and Management, or AppFoundry, is a comprehensive mobile and web application designed to streamline and enhance the operations of production facilities, with a specific focus on foundries. This application covers a wide range of critical functions, including production and production planning, quality control report generation, data collection, and sales and inventory management. Objectives:**  - **Production Management:** AppFoundry will enable users to efficiently plan, track, and manage production processes. It will offer real-time production status updates, production scheduling, and resource allocation.  - **Quality Control:** The application will provide tools for capturing and generating quality control reports, facilitating the identification of defects, and ensuring that products meet industry standards.  - **Data Collection:** AppFoundry will allow users to collect and store essential data about the production process, material usage, and employee performance. It will facilitate data-driven decision-making.  - **Sales and Inventory Management:** The app will provide a seamless solution for managing sales orders, tracking inventory levels, and automating reordering processes to prevent stockouts.  **3. Key Features:**  - **Production Planning and Tracking:** Create production schedules, allocate resources, and monitor progress in real-time.  - **Quality Control Reporting:** Record and generate reports on quality control inspections, facilitating immediate corrective actions.  - **Data Collection:** Collect data on material consumption, machine performance, and employee efficiency for analysis and process improvement.  - **Sales and Order Management:** Track sales orders, manage customer relationships, and automate inventory updates.  - **Inventory Management:** Keep real-time inventory levels, set reorder points, and manage stock efficiently.  - **User Roles and Permissions:** Different levels of access for managers, workers, and quality control teams.  - **Mobile and Web Access:** Access AppFoundry on mobile devices for real-time data collection on the shop floor and on the web for management and reporting.  - **Notifications and Alerts:** Receive alerts for low inventory, quality issues, and production delays.  **4. Expected Benefits:**  - **Improved Efficiency:** Streamline production processes and reduce manual paperwork, leading to increased productivity.  - **Enhanced Quality Control:** Identify and rectify quality issues swiftly, reducing defects and rework.  - **Data-Driven Decision-Making:** Utilize historical data to make informed decisions and optimize operations.  - **Cost Savings:** Reduce excess inventory, prevent stockouts, and lower production costs.  - **Increased Customer Satisfaction:** Deliver products on time with consistent quality.  **5. Implementation and Timeline:**  The project will be completed in a streamlined 5-week timeline:  - **Week 1:** Requirements gathering and system design. - **Week 2:** Application development. - **Week 3:** Quality control and data collection modules development. - **Week 4:** Sales and inventory management modules development. - **Week 5:** Testing, debugging, performance optimization, deployment, training, and support.  **6. Project Team:**  The project team will consist of developers, quality control experts, data analysts, and project managers.  **7. Budget and Resources:**  A detailed budget and resource plan will be established during the project initiation phase.  **8. Conclusion:**  The Application for Integrated Production and Management (AppFoundry) aims to revolutionize production facility management by providing an integrated solution for production, quality control, data collection, and sales and inventory management. This streamlined project will significantly improve the efficiency, quality, and overall performance of production facilities in just 5 weeks, making it an invaluable tool for businesses in the production industry.

I have a project in advanced data analysis (astronomy) : Rapid Oscillations in an HST Lightcurve of the Dwarf Nova OY Car: A cataclysmic variable star is a close binary system with a white dwarf primary and a late-type main- sequence secondary star. The binary separation is so small (the orbit period is only a few hours) that the companion star flls its Roche lobe and transfers gas to an accretion disk around the white dwarf. If the system is viewed at a high inclination (close to the orbit plane), an eclipse is seen at binary phase 0 as the companion star passes in front of the white dwarf and accretion disk. Cataclysmic variables sometimes exhibit \quasi-periodic" oscillations (QPOs) in their brightness. The QPO periods are of order 10 seconds, close to the Kepler period of the white dwarf. An ultraviolet lightcurve obtained with the Hubble Space Telescope records an eclipse of the accretion disk around a white dwarf star in the cataclysmic variable star OY Car. The goal of the project is to use the data analysis techniques learned in the lecture course to search for QPOs in the lightcurve. Suggested steps of analysis: 1. Plot the lightcurve 2. High-pass filter the lightcurve to eliminate the eclipse. 3. Determine frequency sampling. 4. Perform a periodogram analysis. 5. Determine the period 6. Determine the amplitude 7. Are other QPOs present? 8. Are there phase or amplitude changes?

job_title: Video Recognition App for Football Game Analysis - Phase 3 & 4
job_description: Overview  This specification outlines the requirements for a video recognition app designed to analyze football games by recognizing player movements and providing in-depth game analysis. The app is intended for use by football coaches, analysts, and enthusiasts to gain insights into player performance, team tactics, and overall game dynamics.   Functional Requirements  1. Video Input The app should support the import of football game videos in various formats (e.g., MP4, AVI, MKV). Users should be able to capture video footage directly using the device's camera. 2. Player Detection and Tracking The app should detect and track players throughout the video. It should be able to distinguish between players from both teams and assign unique identifiers. Tracking should be accurate and robust, even in the presence of occlusions or players leaving and re-entering the frame. 3. Event Recognition The app should recognize various football events, such as passes, tackles, goals, fouls, and offside situations. It should timestamp and annotate these events in the video timeline. 4. Tactical Analysis Provide tools to create and analyze heatmaps showing player positioning and movement on the field. Support for drawing and annotating tactical diagrams on the video to explain strategies and tactics. 5. Statistics and Metrics Calculate and display key player and team statistics, such as distance covered, speed, and pass completion rates. Offer insights into player and team performance based on the collected data. 6. Video Analysis Allow users to slow down, speed up, or pause the video for a frame-by-frame analysis. Support side-by-side video comparison to analyze player movements and tactics in a split-screen view. 7. Data Export Enable users to export the analyzed data, annotated videos, and tactical diagrams for further use and sharing. 8. User Interface Design an intuitive and user-friendly interface that allows users to interact with the video and analysis tools efficiently. Support both desktop and mobile devices for flexibility in use. Non-functional Requirements 1. Performance The app should provide real-time or near-real-time analysis, even for longer football games. Ensure smooth playback and responsiveness during video analysis. 2. Accuracy Player detection and event recognition should be highly accurate, minimizing false positives and negatives. 3. Security Implement strong security measures to protect user-generated content and data, especially when exporting analysis results. 4. Compatibility Ensure the app works on a variety of devices and operating systems, including Windows, macOS, iOS, and Android. 5. Scalability The app should be able to handle large video files and long-duration football games without performance degradation. 6. Customization Provide options for users to customize the analysis parameters, such as event recognition rules and data presentation. Technical Stack The app should use a combination of computer vision, machine learning, and video processing techniques. Technologies like OpenCV, deep learning frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow or PyTorch), and video codecs should be considered. Testing Rigorous testing should be conducted to ensure the accuracy and robustness of player detection, event recognition, and analysis features. Maintenance and Support Regular updates and bug fixes to maintain the app's reliability and compatibility with new devices and formats. Documentation Provide comprehensive documentation for users and developers to understand how to use the app and integrate it with other systems. Conclusion This specification outlines the key features and requirements for a video recognition app focused on analyzing football games. By meeting these requirements, the app aims to provide valuable insights to football coaches, analysts, and enthusiasts for improved player and team performance analysis. job_title: Power BI Dashboard for Excel Budget Model -- 2
job_description: Seeking a skilled Power BI developer to design a dynamic dashboard for our budget, which is managed in Excel. The goal is to provide insightful visualizations, data connections, and real-time updates to our budget model.  We want to create a comprehensive Power BI dashboard that visualizes key financial data from the following tabs within our budget model:  Income_BU_IFRS: Visualizations should provide insights into income projections under IFRS accounting standards, including revenue sources, growth trends, and potential variations.  Finex_BU_IFRS: We aim to visualize financial expenses, interest rates, and financial income under IFRS to monitor the financial performance of the business.  OPEX_BU_IFRS: This tab will be the basis for visualizing operational expenses, cost breakdowns, and cost management strategies under IFRS accounting.  Income_bu_cash: Visualizing cash-based income data, including cash flow, receivables, and income recognition, to manage liquidity effectively.  Finex_bu_cash: Focusing on cash-based financial income and expenses, interest rates, and their impact on cash flow.  Opex_BU_CASH: Highlighting cash-based operational expenses, cost controls, and cash flow management.  Macro Assumptions: Visualizing macroeconomic assumptions that underpin our budget, such as inflation rates, exchange rates, and other external factors affecting our financial projections.  Our goal is to provide a holistic view of our budget performance, enabling us to monitor the financial health of our organization, make informed decisions, and ensure alignment with our budgeting objectives.   Key Requirements: - Skills Required: Power BI, Excel, data modeling, data transformation, DAX, dashboard design, financial data analysis - Visualize budget breakdown: The dashboard should display key financial data from our budget model - Real-time updates: The dashboard should update automatically as the data in the Excel model changes, providing real-time insights.  Given the tabs in our budget model, here are some recommended types of visualizations for each:  Income_BU_IFRS: Bar Charts or Column Charts: Show revenue sources and trends over time. Line Charts: Visualize revenue growth and comparisons. Pie Charts: Display the composition of income from different sources. Treemaps: Illustrate income hierarchy.  Finex_BU_IFRS: Stacked Bar Charts: Display financial expenses and their components. Area Charts: Highlight the trend in financial income over time. Waterfall Charts: Show the impact of different financial factors on the overall budget.  OPEX_BU_IFRS: Stacked Column Charts: Illustrate the composition of operational expenses. Heatmaps: Visualize expense patterns over categories and time. Scatter Plots: Analyze correlations between different expense items.  Income_bu_cash: Cash Flow Waterfall Charts: Show how cash-based income contributes to overall cash flow. Area Charts: Depict the trend in cash income. Donut Charts: Highlight the proportion of cash income from various sources.  Finex_bu_cash: Cash Flow Statement: Visualize the impact of financial expenses and income on cash flow. Line Charts: Track cash-based financial trends.  Opex_BU_CASH: Stacked Column Charts: Display cash-based operational expenses by category. Heatmaps: Show expense patterns over time. Pareto Charts: Identify the most significant cash-based expense categories.  Macro Assumptions: Line Charts: Track the trends of macroeconomic assumptions over time. Scatter Plots: Analyze correlations between assumptions and financial performance. Remember to make the visualizations interactive by adding filters, slicers, and drill-through options so users can explore the data more deeply.  If you have the skills and experience to create an interactive and visually appealing Power BI dashboard, please submit your proposal your estimated timeline.

Price low and its corresponding time. Last price and its corresponding time. Requirement:  You have a "categories" table with three columns: "id," "name," and "main_category_id," containing 118,118 rows. The "id" field represents the category's unique identifier, the "name" field contains the category's English name, and the "main_category_id" is currently empty (NULL), serving as a foreign key referencing the yet-to-be-populated "main_categories" table.  Here's an example of the "categories" table content:  id	name	main_category_id 1	attributed-no-source	NULL 2	best	NULL 6	out-of-control	NULL 8	worst	NULL 9	dance	NULL Notably, the "name" column contains whole words or hyphenated words.  Additionally, there is an empty "main_categories" table with two columns: "id" and "name."  Task:  Your task is to cluster the categories, find meaningful cluster names that reflect the grouped categories, populate the "main_categories" table with these cluster names, and establish foreign key relationships between the "main_categories" and "categories" tables using the "id" column of the "main_categories" table and the "main_category_id" column of the "categories" table. To maintain consistency, the cluster names should be in lowercase, whether they consist of whole words or hyphenated words.  Additional Points:  1. It is crucial that the main categories you create accurately reflect the content and meaning of the categories contained within each cluster. This ensures that the clustering is intuitive and beneficial.  2. While creating the main categories, please aim to keep the number of main categories reasonable. We want to maintain a balance between granularity and simplicity.  Acceptance Criteria (AC):  AC1: The "main_categories" table must contain the primary category clusters. For example:  id	name 1	love 2	life 3	friendship 4	inspiration 5	wisdom 6	relationships ...	... AC2: The "main_category_id" column in the "categories" table should contain the ID of the cluster (acting as a foreign key referencing the "id" of the "main_categories" table). This should be applied to all 118,118 rows in the "categories" table. For example:  id	name	main_category_id 3	life	2 4	love	1 7	truth	5 19	philosophy	5 20	friendship	3 23	marriage	6 33	friends	3 50	desire	1 52	honesty	5 55	passion	1 56	reality	2 57	relationships	6 ...	...	...

Project Documentation: Motel Management System/Web App    Project Proposal Project Overview: The project aims to develop a comprehensive motel management system and web application to facilitate room bookings, reservations, check-in/check-out processes, meal plans, and pricing management. This system will enhance customer experience and streamline motel operations.  Project Objectives: 1. Create a user-friendly web for customers to book rooms and book if meals included (B, L, D). 2. Develop a system for staff to manage reservations, including the ability to reserve rooms and cancel bookings. 3. Implement pricing and billing management for various room categories and meal plans. 4. Ensure data security, and scalability. 5. Provide a dashboard for staff and admin to monitor room occupancy, meals, and reservations.  Scope Definition  In-scope: - Room booking and reservation management. - Check-in and check-out processes. - Meal plan selection, Breakfast, Lunch, & Dinner. - Pricing management for different room categories and meal plans. - User roles (customers, staff, admin). - Data security and compliance. - Dashboard to monitor room occupancy, meals, and reservations.    Out-of-scope: - Payment gateway integration. All Payments will be paid in the inn intself  Requirements Specification  Functional Requirements:  - User Roles: - Customer - Staff (reception, housekeeping) - Admin  - Customer Functions: - Room booking - Reservation management - Meal plan selection - Review reservations and invoices - Feedback submission  - Staff Functions: - Reserve rooms for customers - Cancel reservations (pending admin approval) - Check-in and check-out management - Room assignment - Meal plan assignment - Pricing adjustments    - Admin Functions: - User management - Reporting and analytics - Pricing configuration - System maintenance - Approval of reservation cancellations   Non-Functional Requirements: - Data Security: Implement secure data storage and transmission. - Scalability: Design for future growth and feature additions. - User Experience: Ensure an intuitive and responsive user interface. - Compliance: Adhere to data privacy.  Use Cases and User Stories  Use Cases: - Customer makes a room reservation. - Staff checks in a customer. - Admin adds a new staff member. - Staff reserves a room. - Staff cancels a reservation (pending admin approval). - Customer selects a meal plan during the reservation process. - Staff add meal plans to customers.      User Stories: - As a customer, I want to easily book a room online. - As an admin, I want to manage user accounts and staff. - As a customer, I want to choose a meal plan during the reservation process. - As a staff member, I want to monitor room occupancy and reservations on a dashboard.  Data Model and Schema Database Tables: - Customers - Rooms - Reservations - Meals - Pricing - Users - Dashboard data (room occupancy, meals, reservations)  Project Timeline: 15 days  Milestones: 1. Requirements Analysis and Design 2. Development and Testing 3. Deployment and User Training 4. Maintenance and Ongoing Support  Cost Estimation Project Budget: [Insert budget estimate here]    Risk Assessment - Data breaches and security vulnerabilities. - Scope creep and changing requirements. - Technical issues and system downtime. - Staff resistance to system adoption.  Conclusion  This project documentation serves as the foundation for the development of the motel management system/web app. It outlines the project's objectives, scope, requirements, and key considerations. It will guide the development team and stakeholders throughout the project's lifecycle.

Incorporating the functionalities of user management and controlled data sharing with expiry into your Apache IoTDB CRUD API proposal adds another layer of sophistication. Here's how to integrate these requirements:  User Management Capabilities:  The API should include the ability to create, modify, and delete user accounts. This involves interfacing with IoTDB's user management system. Implementation of role-based access control (RBAC) to define different levels of access and permissions for various user roles. The developer should ensure secure handling of user credentials and implement best practices in authentication and authorization. Data Sharing with Expiry:  The API must allow for the controlled sharing of data. This includes generating temporary access for external users or systems, with the ability to set expiry dates for this access. Mechanisms to track and manage shared data access, ensuring that access is automatically revoked after the set expiry time. Options for users to extend, modify, or revoke data sharing permissions as needed. Security and Compliance:  Emphasize the importance of security, especially since the API involves user management and data sharing. Ensure compliance with relevant data privacy regulations (like GDPR, HIPAA, etc.) in the context of user data and shared information. Implement logging and auditing features to monitor user activities and data access/sharing for security and compliance purposes. API Design Considerations:  The API design should be intuitive and user-friendly, facilitating easy management of users and shared data. Documentation should include clear instructions and examples for managing users and sharing data with expiry. Scalability and Performance:  The API should be scalable to accommodate a growing number of users and an increasing volume of data sharing requests. Ensure that the implementation of these features does not significantly impact the performance of the IoTDB system. Testing and Quality Assurance:  Rigorous testing of the user management and data sharing features, including testing for security vulnerabilities. Validate the functionality of expiry mechanisms and access controls in various scenarios. Post-Deployment Support:  Offer post-deployment support for a defined period to address any issues that arise with user management or data sharing functionalities.  Skills and experience required: - Advanced expertise in Apache IOTDB CRUD API - Strong knowledge of data analysis techniques and algorithms - Experience in developing APIs for data analysis - Ability to deliver high-quality work within tight deadlines

job_title: Expert Data Analyst needed for large database
job_description: Short-Term Excel Specialist Needed for Quick Data Analysis  ***Important: This task must be completely finished by 4PM Pacific Standard Time so please do not apply unless you are able to accommodate that timeline***  Looking for an Excel expert for a brief engagement involving data analysis. Ideal candidate should be:  Proficient in handling and analyzing large datasets in Excel. Detail-oriented with a keen eye for accuracy. Experienced in using advanced Excel features, such as pivot tables and complex formulas.  Project Details: I will be giving you the parameters in real time as we move through the data Sample Data Attached  Duration: A few hours . Objective: Analyze provided datasets and extract key insights. Deliverables: Comprehensive Excel report based on analysis.  Here are the first set of instructions for your reference- this was written with the expectation that I wil be available in real time for any questions and to approve each step.  Actual Project Objectives' Create 3 new Col.  1. Total buyers represent 2. Total sellers represented 3. Total transactions (add 1&2)  Rank all the agents by total transactions  Separate the list into two pieces  1. Agents that have represented 9 or more total buyers and sellers  And any agents who may have less than nine total but have represented at least three in 2023  Any agents that don't meet either of these criteria are to be removed from the main list and placed on a separate list.  Let's keep our 9+ agents, which I'll refer to you as veterin agents, separate from the "three in 2023" which I'll call the rookie agents from now on.  So now turning our focus to the veteran agents list. I want to get and indication of which price point they deal in.  So for each of their 9+ transactions, there's a column called "closed price" and we're just going to take the average of all of their closed prices... In other words add up the close prices for each of their transactions, regardless of whether they represented the buyer or seller and divide by the number of transactions. if they happen to represent the buyer and the seller count that close price twice for the purposes of the average.  Then add a new column to the master spreadsheet called average sale price.  Next we're going to focus just on the transactions where an agent represented the seller and for this metric, we are only including agents who have represented at least six sellers in total.  So for each of their 6+ seller representing transactions, we are going to take the close price and we're going to subtract The dollar amount that is in the "concessions amount" Column and then we're going to divide that number by the original list price which will leave us with a number like .9 or .95 which we'll call "percentage of list price" Then we're going to take an average by adding each value and dividing by the number of transactions...  Finally will create a column called average percentage of list price which will reflect the average  If you're efficient, skilled, and available for a quick turnaround, I am looking forward to speaking to you.  Thank you very much for you time and consideration,  Steve

I am looking for a skilled developer to create an arbitrage bot that will trade on the Binance cryptocurrency exchange. The bot will be developed using Python as the preferred programming language.  Skills and experience required for this project: - Strong proficiency in Python programming language - Experience in developing trading bots for cryptocurrency exchanges - Knowledge of arbitrage strategies and trading algorithms - Familiarity with the Binance exchange API - Ability to handle high-risk trading strategies and manage potential losses  The ideal candidate should have a proven track record in developing successful arbitrage bots, with a focus on high-risk, high-profit trading strategies. They should be able to effectively utilize the Binance exchange API to execute trades and monitor market conditions. Strong programming skills in Python are essential for developing the bot's functionality and implementing the desired trading strategy.  The goal of this project is to create an arbitrage bot. I am looking for it to have an interface I can interact with on a MAC. The features that I would like implemented in this bot would be as follows Multiple Exchange Integration: Supports a vast number of exchanges globally, allowing for a wider net to spot opportunities. High-frequency Trading: Executes trades at extremely high speeds to capitalize on minuscule price discrepancies that exist for mere seconds. Liquidity Checks: Ensures there's enough volume on both the buy and sell side to execute a trade without causing slippage. Fee Calculation: Incorporates trading and withdrawal fees into profit calculations to ensure genuine profitability. Transfer Time Consideration: Evaluates the time it takes to transfer assets between exchanges, especially critical for crypto arbitrage. Triangular Arbitrage: Looks for discrepancies within a single exchange, trading across three different assets to exploit price differences. Historical Data Analysis: Uses past price data to predict future arbitrage opportunities or to refine trading strategies. Machine Learning & AI: Implements algorithms that learn from past trades and market conditions to optimize future trades. Customizable Trading Strategies: Allows users to define specific parameters or conditions for trades. Risk Management Tools: Features like stop-loss, take-profit, or withdrawal limits to minimize potential losses. Spread Analysis: Monitors the bid-ask spread to find opportunities and evaluate liquidity. Real-time Analytics Dashboard: A user-friendly interface showing live profit/loss, open trades, and potential arbitrage opportunities. Notification System: Instant alerts (via SMS, email, or apps) about arbitrage opportunities or bot activities. Fail-Safes: Mechanisms to halt trading during extreme market volatility or unfavorable conditions. VPN & Security Features: To ensure safe trading, especially in regions where certain exchanges might be restricted. Automated Withdrawals & Deposits: Automate the process of moving assets between exchanges when required.  I am also looking to have it be capable of Decentralized Exchange Support: Incorporate trading on decentralized exchanges (DEXs) to expand the arbitrage landscape. Cloud Integration: Host the bot on cloud platforms like AWS or Google Cloud for higher uptime and scalability. Parallel Execution: Run multiple trading strategies or operate on multiple pairs/exchanges concurrently. Sentiment Analysis: Integrate with news sites or social media to gauge market sentiment, which can influence price movements. Smart Order Routing: Automatically determine the best trade route across multiple exchanges to maximize profit. Backtesting Suite: Test strategies against historical data to refine and optimize them before live trading. Trading Signal Integration: Incorporate signals from popular trading platforms or indicators. Whale Watching: Track large (whale) trades and adjust strategy based on these movements. Tax Reporting & Analytics: Automated generation of tax reports and detailed analytics on trades. Cold Storage Integration: Automatically move funds to cold storage for increased security after trades. API Rate Limit Management: Smart algorithms to manage and adapt to API call rate limits imposed by exchanges. Dynamic Fee Structures: Adjust trades based on changing fee structures or promotional periods on exchanges. Gas Price Optimization: For crypto bots, optimize Ethereum gas prices for timely and cost-effective transactions. Advanced Graphical Interface: A more detailed UI/UX with customizable dashboards, charts, and analytics tools. User Role Management: If the bot is used by a team, manage roles and permissions for different users. Plug-in or Extension Support: Allow third-party developers to create plugins or extensions to enhance bot functionality. Integrated VPN: For added security and to bypass geo-restrictions. Auto-scaling: The ability for the bot to automatically scale its operations based on market activity or detected opportunities. Periodic Self-audits: The bot can self-audit its operations to ensure accuracy and detect any anomalies. Mobile App Integration: Control and monitor the bot on-the-go with a dedicated mobile app. Even with these advanced features, remember the golden rule: always keep an eye on your bot. No system is infallible, and it's essential to stay updated with market conditions and be ready to intervene manually if necessary.  If the mobile app portion is to complex I understand and you can leave that out. I am willing to pay very well if you are able to get this done. Hopefully in a decent time frame :}

A Research Specialist is a skilled professional who plays a pivotal role in the field of research. Their primary responsibilities encompass the planning, execution, and management of research projects. They are adept at gathering, organizing, and analyzing data, employing a wide range of research methodologies and tools. Research Specialists are detail-oriented and possess strong analytical skills, enabling them to draw meaningful conclusions from complex information.  These specialists collaborate with interdisciplinary teams, academia, or private organizations to contribute to the advancement of knowledge, problem-solving, or product development. They often design experiments, surveys, or data collection processes, ensuring data integrity and adherence to ethical standards. Furthermore, they are proficient in statistical analysis software and possess the ability to interpret statistical findings accurately.  Effective communication is a vital aspect of their role, as Research Specialists are responsible for presenting research findings through reports, presentations, or publications. They may also engage in grant writing and proposal development to secure funding for research initiatives.  In summary, a Research Specialist is a highly skilled professional who conducts research, analyzes data, and communicates findings, making a significant contribution to the progress of various fields, from academia to industry.

job_title: Dev Tool for Planning Game Progression (Web App/Desktop App)
job_description: Seeking a developer to create a utilitarian tool designed explicitly for planning and simulating the progression of a tycoon-style game in a development context. In a tycoon game, players navigate through a set path of purchasable items and levels, accumulating in-game currency and points to buy items, which in turn impact their resource accumulation rate. All players follow the same purchase path, though not all items are compulsory to progress through the game.  The tool's primary function is as a development aid, allowing game designers to meticulously plan, simulate, and analyze different game configurations and progression paths before they are implemented in the actual game. Although this tool will be crucial in planning and simulating game progression, it is not intended to interface with the game or game engine directly.  Core Objectives of the Tool:  Data Input Interface: A basic UI that allows the input of game parameters such as purchasable items, costs, and rewards. Simulation Engine: Algorithms to simulate game progression, calculating crucial metrics like completion time and item ROI, based on input parameters. Visualizations: Basic graphical representations of simulation results to assist in analyzing game progression and item efficiency. Data Export/Import: Functionalities to export and import game configurations and simulation results.  Technical Specifications:  Platform: Web or Windows application. Frontend: A minimal, functional UI for data input and visualization. Backend: Algorithms and logic to perform simulations and calculate metrics. Data Storage: Mechanisms for storing, retrieving, and sharing game configurations and results.  Additional Requirements: It is pivotal that the tool allows for most operations (such as simulations and data input/output) to be performed WITHOUT utilizing the UI, ensuring that the tool can be utilized in an automated manner, or integrated into other development workflows.  Additional Notes: The UI does not need to be polished; however, it must be functional and straightforward to use. The tool is expected to act as a behind-the-scenes development assistant, enabling a comprehensive understanding and analysis of various design decisions before they are actualized in the game.  Your expertise will contribute to streamlining the game development process and enhancing player experience by providing a solid, reliable simulation, and analysis tool. This tool leverages Puppeteer, a high-level API library for headless Chrome or Chromium in Node.js, and TypeScript, a statically typed JavaScript superset, which ensures more maintainable and scalable code.  The primary feature of this system is automating the job application process. Puppeteer will navigate the Job Portal platform, navigate thru every job URL from list, and automatically apply to these jobs using the default profile.  After the job application process, all the extracted job data and application statuses will be structured and stored in a database for future reference and analysis. The system will use a Python API for reading and writing data to this database. Below fields should be extracted  - Job Title - Job URL - Location - Posted Date (if available) - Job Type (e.g., Full-Time, Contract-to-Hire) - Rate/Pay (if available) - isSubmitted - isFailed  The end goal of this project is to provide job seekers and HR professionals with a tool that makes the job application process more efficient, saving them considerable time and effort.

job_description: I need help with a math project involving cricket team analysis. I have 22 players labeled P1 to P22. From these, I need to pick 11 players for a cricket team. I have a sheet named "TEAM A" with all possible combinations of 11 players in rows labeled PC1, PC2, and so on, up to PC705432 (total 705432 rows). Each row represents a different combination of players. This sheet is organized in a table named "PossibleSet."  In another sheet named "Team B," I have historical user data. This sheet contains information about users who have created cricket teams of 11 players using different combinations from the 22 players. Each user's team is listed in rows labeled CT-1, CT-2, up to CT-97559. This sheet is organized in a table named "UserData." The size of this table varies as it's based on actual contest data.  I want to determine how many players from each possible combination in the "PossibleSet" table match the players in each team listed in the "UserData" table. I've already used simple SUM and COUNTIF formulas in column O of the "Team B" sheet to count the matching players in each user's team against P1.  Here's what I need to accomplish: -	Calculate the number of matching players between each possible combination (e.g., PC1) and each user's team (e.g., CT-1, CT-2). However, there are over 700,000 possible combinations, which Excel can't handle in columns. I need a different approach/format to perform this analysis. -	Identify which combination sets are popular among users, considering a range of maximum and minimum players. I want to find out which combinations are less favored or ignored. -	Create a dynamic solution that allows me to change the data in the "Team B" sheet to analyze multiple user datasets. I need a complete sheet with formulas, results, and explanations so that I can easily filter and interpret the results later.  In essence, I'm looking for a more efficient way to analyze the matching players between possible combinations and user teams, while also understanding the popularity of specific combination sets and their player count ranges. I'd greatly appreciate assistance in setting up a dynamic analysis sheet with formulas and explanations.  I prefer to use - Excel - may be power query, or any other method. We can supply data in excel, csv.  I am open for any other Free /open source tool including BI Tools,  PHP/ Python program as well.  Let me share your proposal and time required

job_title: Excel Lead Tracker with Advanced Analytics
job_description: We are seeking skilled Excel professionals to create a comprehensive and user-friendly Excel spreadsheet for tracking and analyzing leads generated by our sales team. This tool will help us monitor our lead generation and sales performance more effectively.  The Excel spreadsheet should have the following features:  * Time Period Tracking: The ability to track the number of leads made on a weekly, monthly, and yearly basis. The spreadsheet should automatically update totals as new data is entered.  * Disposition of Leads: We need to categorize the leads based on their status. The categories include sold, canceled, not home, and pitched. The spreadsheet should allow users to enter leads into these categories.  * Lead Conversion Metrics: Calculate and display the percentage of leads that fall into each disposition category (sold, canceled, not home, pitched). This should be displayed in a dashboard or report format for easy viewing.  * Salesperson Tracking: Ability to add salespeople and associate leads with specific salespeople. We want to track the performance of individual salespeople, including their total leads, leads sold, and closing ratios.  * Source Tracking: Ability to categorize leads based on their source (e.g., online marketing, referrals, cold calls). This will help us understand where our most effective leads are coming from.  * Closing Ratio: Calculate the closing ratio for each salesperson and for the overall team. The closing ratio is defined as the number of leads sold divided by the total number of leads pitched. Display this as a percentage.  * Data Visualization: Provide charts and graphs to visually represent lead data, disposition of leads, salesperson performance, lead sources, and closing ratios. The charts should automatically update as new data is entered.  * Data Filtering: Users should be able to filter the data to view the metrics for specific time periods, salespeople, lead sources, or lead dispositions.  * User-Friendly Interface: The spreadsheet should be user-friendly and easy to navigate, with clear labels, instructions, and an intuitive design.  * Documentation: Provide a simple user guide that explains how to use the spreadsheet, enter data, and interpret the reports and charts.  Time Tracker Features:  Daily Lead Analysis: The spreadsheet should provide insights into how many leads are generated every day. This will enable the sales team to gauge daily productivity and consistency.  Weekly, Monthly, & Yearly Aggregates: Beyond the daily figures, the tool should automatically compute weekly, monthly, and yearly totals. This will assist in monitoring patterns, detecting high and low lead generation periods, and making strategic decisions based on these insights.  Time of Day Analysis: Understand when leads are primarily created during the day. This information can offer insights into the peak hours for lead generation.  Lead Lifecycle Timestamps: The ability to log the exact date and time when a lead transitions between different stages (e.g., from 'pitched' to 'sold'). This can provide insights into how quickly leads move through the sales funnel.  Data Visualization: Integrate charts that showcase daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly trends. The visual representation should allow users to quickly grasp patterns and changes over time.  Data Filtering & Comparison: Users should be able to filter the time-based data and compare different periods. For instance, compare lead generation in the first quarter of the year to the second quarter.  Automated Alerts: Implement a feature that can notify users if there is an unusual increase or decrease in lead generation during a specific time period.  Incorporating a robust Time Tracker will empower us to make data-driven decisions and strategize our sales efforts more effectively based on when leads are most frequently generated and how they transition over time.  The winning entry will be selected based on the accuracy, ease of use, and visual appeal of the spreadsheet. We are looking for a tool that will help us gain insights into our lead generation and sales processes and ultimately improve our performance.  Thank you

job_title: Retrieval and Organization of Canadian Government Forms
job_description: We are seeking an organized and detail-oriented freelancer to assist us in a comprehensive data collection and validation project. The fundamental goal of this project is to identify all forms used by the federal and provincial governments of Canada where there is a field for the citizen's Social Insurance Number (SIN). This includes any form where it could be possible for the SIN to be present once the form is completed by the citizen.  The project entails the following responsibilities:  Form Retrieval: Your task involves conducting thorough research across various federal, provincial, and territorial government organizations in Canada. This includes visiting websites like Service Canada and others to identify potential forms. Your primary objective is to download specific forms that contain a Social Insurance Number (SIN) field. In addition, if you encounter forms containing other sensitive information such as Health Identification Numbers, Driver's License details, or Passport numbers, these should also be downloaded.  To aid your search, we will provide a Google Sheet that lists typical forms that could contain a SIN. However, this list is not exhaustive or accurate, and you are expected to identify and download all relevant forms not included in the list.  Your research should cover the federal government, all 10 provinces, and the 3 territories of Canada. Here's a comprehensive list of all provinces and territories:  Provinces: - Alberta - British Columbia - Manitoba - New Brunswick - Newfoundland and Labrador - Nova Scotia - Ontario - Prince Edward Island - Quebec (french) - Saskatchewan  Territories: - Northwest Territories - Nunavut - Yukon  We anticipate this task will require extensive online research and a meticulous approach to ensure all relevant forms are captured.  2. Form Validation: Your crucial task is to confirm that each form contains a field for the Social Insurance Number (SIN) that would typically be filled out by the citizen. This step is key to our later analysis of these forms.  3. Test Data Insertion: If a form has multiple SIN fields, each field should be filled with a unique fictitious number. Use the following numbers: "699141008", "691126866", "684711856", "603879560", "261711295", and "105031421". Ensure a different number is used for each SIN field on the same form.  4. File Naming and Organization: Each downloaded file should be renamed with a prefix indicating the specific province (e.g., Alberta_FormName). If the form pertains to the federal level, please prefix it accordingly (e.g., Federal_FormName). Otherwise, the original form name should remain intact from the download.  5. List Updating / Unlisted Files: You must update the Google Sheet accordingly with the forms discovered to contain SIN fields.  6. File Storage and Linking: Upload the renamed files to a Google Drive, organized into folders representing each province or the federal level. Once uploaded, create a hyperlink for each file in the supplied Google Sheet.  At the end of this project, we should have a copy of all governmental forms containing fictitious SINs, and a Google Sheet listing all governmental forms, along with hyperlinks to the actual forms organized in Google Drive.  Example government portal to search forms: - Federal [login to view URL] - Quebec government [login to view URL]!/filter?categories=3156  Numerous forms are accessible on government websites. However, for this project, it's crucial to concentrate specifically on those forms that pertain to individual citizens, as these are most likely to contain Social Insurance Number (SIN) fields.  Expectations: This project is urgent and we expect an immediate start and results. Do not bid unless you can deliver on this project rapidly.  Skills Required:  Proficient in English and French (for the Quebec government) Strong research skills Detail-oriented Proficient in Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive) Familiarity with Canadian governmental organizations would be a bonus

Page 7: Some of this is still boiler plate but it will be necessary to manually enter specific information. Summary and Conclusions - these bullet points will change for each report but there should be a place holder for the conclusions.  Page 9: There should be a drop down to add reference information. The first one always had the date of the inspection. The others could change or more could be added.  Pages 10 to 21: These are the inspection photographs. The program will interact with the Garmin Connect API to fetch training data from the Garmin Connect app. It will authenticate with the API using your provided credentials. The fetched data will include details such as activity type, duration, distance, average heart rate, and other relevant parameters.  Once the data is retrieved, the program will export it to an Excel file. Each new run will be appended as a new row in the Excel file, including the required fields: date, activity type, duration, distance, pace, average heart rate, and any other relevant parameters. The final output will be the generated Excel file containing the training data and the calculated weekly and monthly totals. Excel Export: - The program should export the fetched training data to an Excel file. - Each new run should be appended to the existing Excel file as a new row. - The exported data should include the following fields: date, activity type, duration, distance, pace (time per kilometer), average heart rate, and any other relevant parameters.  4. Weekly Data Analysis: - The program should analyze the weekly data and calculate the following totals: - Total distance covered in the week (in meters or kilometers). - Total duration of all activities in the week (in seconds or minutes). - Time spent in different heart rate zones (e.g., time in zone 1, zone 2, etc.).  5. Monthly Data Analysis: - Similar to the weekly analysis, the program should perform monthly data analysis. - The monthly data should be calculated from the 1st day of the month to the next 1st day of the month.  6. API Integration: - The program should be implemented using the Garmin Connect API to fetch training data. - The API should be integrated with proper authentication and data retrieval endpoints. - Ensure that the code handles API responses correctly and processes the retrieved data accurately.  7. Documentation and Output: - The program should generate an Excel file containing the fetched training data and analysis. - The generated file should be properly organized and labeled with clear column headers. - The weekly and monthly totals should be displayed or printed as output to verify the calculations.

job_title: Markowitz Eff Frontier on 18 mos 30 stock of the DJ
job_description: I'm looking for someone to perform a Markowitz Efficient Frontier analysis on a selection of 30 stocks from the Dow Jones Industrial Average over a period of 18 months. I have a specific list of stocks (30 of the DJ) in mind for the project and I would like the results presented in graph form. This analysis should provide insight into how different combinations of assets can be optimized for maximum return while minimizing risk. The job includes data collection.  The Markowitz Efficient Frontier can be used to calculate the optimal portfolio given a set of financial data, so I'm looking for a professional with a strong background in finance and portfolio optimization to get the job done. o calculate the Markowitz Efficient Frontier for the Dow Jones stocks over the past 18 months, you would need the following data:  Historical daily prices for each of the Dow Jones stocks: You need the daily closing prices for each stock in the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) for the past 18 months. These prices will be used to calculate the stock returns and estimate their statistical properties.  Risk-free rate of return: You would need the risk-free rate of return for the same period. Typically, the risk-free rate is represented by the yield on a government bond, such as the U.S. Treasury bills or bonds with a similar maturity to your investment horizon.  Historical returns for each Dow Jones stock: From the daily prices, you would calculate the historical returns for each stock. The returns are usually calculated as the percentage change in price from one day to the next.  Covariance matrix of returns: With the historical returns for each stock, you can calculate the covariance matrix. The covariance matrix represents the interdependencies or relationships between the returns of different stocks. It is a key input for the Markowitz portfolio optimization.  Expected returns for each Dow Jones stock: You need to estimate the expected returns for each stock. These estimates can be based on historical returns, analysts' forecasts, or other relevant information.  By utilizing these data sets, you can construct the Markowitz Efficient Frontier by considering different combinations of stocks in your portfolio and finding the optimal allocation that maximizes return for a given level of risk or minimizes risk for a given level of return. The efficient frontier is determined by solving the mathematical optimization problem based on the covariance matrix, expected returns, and risk-free rate.

I am looking for a data analyst to help with a descriptive analysis project. The ideal candidate should have experience in developing decision trees using precision tree.  Specifics:  - Type of Data Analysis: Statistical analysis, textual analysis, and visual analysis - Dataset: The client already has a specific dataset in mind - Desired Outcome: The analysis should inform decision-making  Skills and Experience:  - Proficiency in precision tree and decision tree development - Experience in statistical analysis, textual analysis, and visual analysis - Strong analytical skills to identify trends and predict future outcomes - Ability to effectively communicate and present findings to inform decision-making  If you have the skills and experience required for this project, please submit your proposal.  ACTUAL INFORMATION FOR THE PROJECT:  In a summer plagued by drought and heat, many Southern crops are withering in the field.  Some farmers are continuing to nurture their crops and hope for the best.  Others have given up hope and decided to plow their crops under.  Farmers already have $50/acre invested in seed, fertilizer, and labor.  Continuing to nurture the crops will require another $15/acre.  Abandoning the crops will cost nothing additional.  The outcome will depend on the weather.  Meterologists predict a 70% chance of favorable weather conditions and a 30% chance of unfavorable conditions.  If conditions are good, farmers can expect revenue of $26/acre.  If they are bad, even though the farmer has continued to nurture the crops, they can expect revenue of $12/acre.  Your high school friend and current farmer, Mike McDonald, has asked you to analyze the situation and prepare a report advising him of what he should do.  The information for the project should include the following:  Develop a decision tree for the problem using Precision Tree and use and explain it in a report What is the EMV of the tree? What decision should the farmer take, based on EMV? How much would the probability of favorable weather conditions have to change to alter recommended decision, holding all other information constant? Show a sensitivity analysis that spans this value. How much would the revenue per acre change to alter recommended decision, holding all other information constant? Show a sensitivity analysis that spans this value. What other considerations might Mikey need to take into account in making this decision?  I need to upload a spreadsheet and a report for Mike McDonald.  Here are some hints to keep in mind:  Consider the nature of sunk costs Consider that when you vary the probability of good weather, its complement must also vary Consider that this is a very simple problem where you could sensitivity test the decision using either spreadsheet modeling or PrecisionTree. You can copy the image of your tree for pasting into your report using these steps: right click the decision node choose "Tools" from the resulting menu select "Copy Tree to Clipboard" select "Entire Tree"

job_title: Android-Auto Infotainment Ready APP Development - FUEL abc
job_description: We are looking for an company/Individual providing "Android Auto" application development team to create an Infotainment-ready app called FUEL abc. The app's main focus is to provide advanced navigation features and real-time trip cost tracking for users, with the added requirement of offline functionality.  One of the key features we require is for the app to work seamlessly offline, without internet connectivity. All the necessary data will be stored locally on the infotainment system. Once the system regains internet access, the app will automatically fetch the latest data from the internet and update the local storage. This way, users can access the app and its functionalities even when they don't have an active internet connection.  The app's navigation feature will rely on GNSS (Global Navigation Satellite System) for accurate positioning and guidance. Users will be able to navigate their trips using the app, even in areas with limited or no internet connectivity.  In addition to offline functionality, the app will gather data from various sources. Vehicle information, including speed, fuel level, make/model, engine size, fuel type, and the user's current location using GNSS, will be collected from the infotainment system. Users will also provide additional data such as country/state/city details, start and end locations, log entries, reminders, and emergency contact information for their family members.  To enhance the app's capabilities, we'll integrate the FUEL abc API, which has already been developed in Python. This API will provide essential data such as trip cost, cost per kilometer, mileage, range anxiety, fuel prices, vehicle cost analysis, log book entries, reminders, and trip summaries.  The successful candidate will need to ensure that the app functions seamlessly offline and can access the required data from the local storage on the infotainment system. When an internet connection is available, the app will automatically fetch the latest data. Navigation will primarily rely on GNSS for accurate positioning and guidance.  We want a long term relations, as the list of features may evolve over time. The developer should prioritize user security and compatibility with the Infotainment system. The app should pass all necessary tests for publication on Android Auto.  For a detailed breakdown of the project requirements, please refer to the attached PPT document. It provides a comprehensive list of features for our existing hybrid app.

job_title: Data Analysis, Product Consolidation, and Sales Insights
job_description: We are seeking a skilled freelancer to assist us in analyzing our sales data, consolidating similar products, and extracting valuable insights related to Sales Volume, Sales Trends, Correlations and Associations, and Financing Penetration. The goal is to optimize our product catalog, identify sales patterns, and understand the impact of financing options on customer purchasing behavior.  Project Overview: * Data Analysis and Consolidation: Your first task will involve carefully examining the product names and identify items that are essentially the same but have slight differences in the name due to varying attributes such as length and width. For example, "14k Solid Gold Curb Chain - 20" (Medium) / 1.9mm" and "14k Solid Gold Curb Chain - 20" / 1.9mm" are the same product but listed differently. Develop a set of consolidation criteria that will allow you to combine similar items into one unified entry. The consolidation should be primarily based on the product name, while preserving the length and width attributes. * Sales Volume: Calculate and analyze the sales volume for each consolidated product. Identify the best-selling and least-selling products to prioritize marketing efforts and inventory management. * Sales Trends: Examine sales trends for the consolidated products. Identify any seasonal patterns, upward or downward trends, and significant changes over time. This information will be crucial for demand forecasting and stock planning. * Correlations and Associations: Explore correlations and associations between different products in the consolidated dataset. Identify items that are frequently purchased together and uncover any cross-selling opportunities. * Financing Penetration: Determine the percentage of customers who opted for financing when purchasing items from the consolidated product catalog. Analyze how financing options influence sales volume and customer behavior. Project Deliverables: * Consolidated Dataset: Provide the finalized dataset with similar products consolidated based on the specified criteria. * Sales Insights Report: Present a comprehensive report summarizing the sales insights derived from the data analysis. This report should include sales volume breakdown, sales trends analysis, and any noteworthy correlations and associations between products. * Financing Impact Analysis: Prepare a separate section in the report focusing on the impact of financing options on customer purchasing behavior. Analyze financing penetration rates and its effects on overall sales and customer preferences. * Data Visualization: Create visualizations such as charts, graphs, and tables to effectively communicate the key findings and trends identified in the analysis. * Recommendations: Based on the insights gained from the data analysis, provide actionable recommendations to optimize the product catalog, marketing strategies, and financing options. Requirements: * Proficiency in data analysis and experience working with large datasets. * Strong analytical skills to identify sales patterns and correlations. * Knowledge of statistical methods for data analysis. * Understanding of finance-related data and how financing options impact consumer behavior. * Proficiency in data visualization tools to present findings in an easy-to-understand format.  Note: The freelancer must be able to handle the entire process, from data cleaning and consolidation to deriving valuable insights and presenting them in a well-structured report. We anticipate a thorough analysis that will guide us in making data-driven decisions to enhance our business strategies. Upon completion of the project, we may consider an ongoing collaboration based on the quality of work and the value provided through the insights. If you possess the necessary expertise and are confident in your abilities, we welcome your proposal outlining your approach and estimated timeline for completing this multifaceted project. Feel free to ask any questions or request additional information to aid in your analysis.

job_title: CodRep: Machine Learning on Source Code Competition
job_description: Software engineering/machine learning problem that was launched a few years ago. Full details of the task can be found at [login to view URL], but basically the challenge involves predicting where to insert a specific line into a source code file. i.e. the system takes as input a set of pairs (source code line, source code file), and outputs, for each pair, the predicted line number of the line to be replaced by in the initial source code file.  Unfortunately it's too late to enter the actual competition but this still forms an interesting machine learning problem. There are many different ways of tacking this - e.g. one approach might perform some degree of syntactic and semantic analysis of the inputs before using machine learning, whereas another might adopt a deep learning approach and train a network with hundreds of thousands of examples of maintenance changes.

Data entry is the process of inputting, updating, and organizing various types of data into a computer system or database. This information can be in the form of text, numbers, images, or other media. Data entry plays a crucial role in managing and maintaining accurate records for businesses, organizations, and institutions.  Data entry tasks may include, but are not limited to:  Typing and transcribing documents: Entering information from physical documents, such as forms, surveys, invoices, and handwritten notes, into digital formats.  Database management: Creating, updating, and managing databases, including adding new records, modifying existing ones, and ensuring data integrity.  Data verification: Double-checking the accuracy of data by comparing it with the original source or cross-referencing with other databases.  Data cleansing: Removing or correcting errors, inconsistencies, or duplicate entries to ensure data quality and reliability.  Data conversion: Converting data from one format to another (e.g., from paper-based records to digital format).  Data mining: Extracting specific information or patterns from a large dataset for analysis or reporting.  Data input for online platforms: Uploading product information, customer details, or other data onto e-commerce websites, content management systems, or other online platforms.  Spreadsheet management: Creating and maintaining spreadsheets with various data, such as financial records, inventory lists, or employee information.  Image and document digitization: Converting scanned documents or images into digital formats, making them easily searchable and accessible.  Data categorization and classification: Organizing data into specific categories or groups to facilitate easier retrieval and analysis.  Data entry requires attention to detail and accuracy, as errors can lead to misinformation and potentially impact business operations or decision-making processes. It is often carried out by data entry operators or administrative professionals using specialized software applications or custom databases.  Automation and artificial intelligence have also played a significant role in improving data entry processes, reducing manual efforts, and increasing overall efficiency. However, human oversight and intervention are still essential for ensuring data accuracy and handling complex tasks that require judgment and context.

job_title: Dynamic Excel Data Model for Valorant Player Performance Analysis
job_description: Description:   Project Overview: I am looking to develop a comprehensive Excel data model that will allow us to track and analyze the performance of Valorant players throughout the tournament. The main goal of this project is to create a user-friendly data model that provides valuable insights into player performance. The data model should enable us to easily enter data and visualize player statistics through visually appealing charts, graphs, and sparklines.  Specific Requirements:  Data Structure: The data model should have the following columns to track player performance:  Team Name: The name of the Valorant team to which the player belongs. Player Name: The name of each individual Valorant player. Kills: The number of kills each player has achieved. Matches Played: The total number of matches played by each player. Average Kills per Match: Calculated as the total kills divided by the matches played, representing the average kills per match for each player. Dynamic Functionality: It is crucial that the data model can handle real-time updates and perform calculations based on changing inputs. For example, we should be able to update the data for each player's kills and matches played after each game, and the data model should automatically recalculate the average kills per match for each player accordingly.  Visual Representation: To effectively analyze player performance trends, the data model should incorporate visually appealing charts, graphs, and sparklines. These visual elements will help us understand the players' progress and identify potential statistical advantages.  Data Integration (Optional): If possible, we would like to include real-time data from external sources like PrizePicks prop lines. If the Fiverr agent has experience with data integration, we would appreciate them using Power Query or web APIs to incorporate PrizePicks data into the data model. This will enable us to compare projected stats with actual player performance.  Overall, we are looking for a data model that simplifies the process of tracking Valorant player statistics and provides actionable insights for strategic decision-making during the tournament. The model should be easy to use, visually engaging, and capable of handling real-time updates to keep us informed with the latest player data.

Sure, I can provide some information on data entry as a project.  Data entry involves the process of transcribing information from one format to another, typically from physical documents to digital databases or spreadsheets. This type of work is often performed by individuals or teams with a high level of accuracy and attention to detail, as errors in data entry can have serious consequences for businesses and organizations.  The goal of a data entry project is to accurately and efficiently transfer data from one source to another, ensuring that the information is organized and easily searchable for future reference. Data entry projects may involve a wide range of information, including customer or client data, financial records, medical records, survey responses, and more.  One common type of data entry project involves digitizing handwritten forms or documents. For example, a company may have a large collection of paper-based customer surveys that need to be input into a digital database for analysis. This can involve manually typing the responses into a spreadsheet or using specialized software to scan and recognize the text.  Data entry projects can also involve verifying and cleaning up existing data. This can include identifying and correcting errors in data sets, updating outdated information, or removing duplicate entries. This type of work requires a keen eye for detail and a good understanding of data management best practices.  In some cases, data entry projects may require specialized knowledge or skills. For example, a medical billing company may need data entry specialists who are familiar with medical terminology and coding. Similarly, a financial services company may require data entry specialists who are proficient in accounting software and terminology.  Data entry projects can be performed by individuals working from home, or by teams working in an office environment. The key to success in data entry is accuracy, speed, and attention to detail. It is also important to have good communication skills
