We basically want to encrypt the video on the encoded frame level, ie (I, P, B frames) we want to bit manipulate frames before they go into the Huffman encoded stream. A custom encoding will be applied to the frames then they go to Huffman and the reverse on the decoder. Import the file and encode the frames using Mpeg Inside the Mpeg decoder, we should isolate the packets and isolate frames of the three types we should then encrypt the frames with the encryption function then we should have the encrypted data repackaged the MPEG encoder should finish the job and continue with the process of writing them to the file The decryption should follow the same approach; then put it in reverse.  Extras: The encoding key will be a quadratic chaotic map (this is very easy the achieve once we have an entry into the raw encoded frames before Huffman) basically the map is the key that we shift bits according to so no worries there.  Simulation program for security analysis and output analysis 1)Specification of the tested frames 2) a) Histogram analysis b) pixel distribution for the three RGB channels of the evaluated frame (for the original frame and encrypted frame) 3) Correlation coefficient analysis between plain and encrypted frames. 4)Entropy analysis 5)Peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) 6) Number of pixel change rate (NPCR) 7)Unified average changing intensity (UACI) & MSE 8) Structural similarity (SSIM) index 9)Differential measurement using different control parameter measuring (MSE, UACI, NPCR)& Differential measurement using different initial condition measuring ((MSE, UACI,NPCR) 10)Encryption speed analysis 11) Attack script Inputs - A video file - attack type and intensity ( eg salt and pepper attack) Output - A video file of the same format with the desired attack applied

Below, you'll find specifications for the project. Please review them and provide us with a quote for your services and which technology / services you suggest.  Project Requirements 1. Website Crawler: We need a website crawler that can crawl an entire website based on an inputted domain name. This crawler should be able to navigate and extract information from all the pages on the website, including both HTML pages and PDFs.  2. Word Identification: The system should be able to identify specific words used on the website. We will provide a list of approximately 100 words. The system should be able to generate a list of URLs where one or more of these words are used. Each URL should be given a score based on the frequency and variety of these words on the page.  3. Contextual Information: Every time a word from the list is found, the system should display the word in context. This means displaying a text snippet with 100 characters before and after the word.  4. Data Analysis: The system should provide an overall analysis of how many times words from our list are used across the entire website. Additionally, it should display what percentage of the total words on the website come from our list.  5. Data Storage and Comparison: All results must be saved per website. However the full crawled content does not need to be saved. Just processed. The system should allow us to re-run the crawl and compare the results with those of the previous crawl.  6. Machine Learning Application: Optimally, the system should utilize machine learning. This will allow an admin user to categorize a found word and its context as either "problematic" or "unproblematic". Over time, the system should improve its ability to score these word-context pairs, rating them on a scale from 1-10 based on their "likeliness to be problematic".  Deliverables Initially, we're looking for a technical "proof of concept". It's acceptable for the initial version to have some workarounds or minor limitations.  We looking for a solution that utilized existing frameworks to services in order to save time and increase quality. Could be services such as OpenAI, Huggingface or other relevant services  If the proof of concept is successful, we would like the option to further develop the tool into a more polished product. Please include your estimate for the proof of concept as well as any thoughts you have on potential future development in your quote.  Looking forward to hearing from you.  Skills and experience required: - Experience in web crawling and data scraping - Knowledge of natural language processing and text analysis - Proficiency in programming languages such as Python and Java, HTML, CSS, JS   Best Regards, Henry

job_title: Customizable Multilingual Job Board System with Advanced ATS and Automation Features
job_description: We're seeking talented freelancers to develop a comprehensive job board system that meets the specific requirements of our client. The objective is to create a platform where job seekers can apply for posted positions, while administrators can efficiently manage applicants and job listings. We have identified several open-source scripts that can be customized to fulfill the project's needs. The key features expected from the system:  Job Board Functionality: A customizable job board script that allows administrators to post job listings and manage applicant submissions effectively.  Customizable Questionnaires: The ability for administrators to modify job-specific questionnaires and include additional questions in the application form.  Export to Excel: The option for administrators to export applicant information, including their answers, to an Excel file for further analysis.  Live ATS & Statistics: Real-time statistics and reporting capabilities to provide administrators with quick insights into stored data, including applicant information and recruitment metrics.  Quick Accept/Reject & Automated Email Communication: The ability for administrators to easily accept or reject applicants with a single click, accompanied by automated email notifications to the applicants.  Document Management: An integrated system that allows administrators to add additional documents to applicant profiles.  Automatic PDF Generation: The system should automatically generate a PDF for each published job, providing a unique URL that is easily accessible to job seekers. The PDF should have a visually appealing layout and present the job details effectively.  Multilingual Support: The system should support multiple languages, including German, to cater to a diverse user base.  You're welcome to choose other scripts (if so, please introduce them) but based on above requirements, the following open-source scripts have been identified as solutions:  Job Board Functionality: For the job board functionality, we have considered Jobberbase and evaluated its flexibility and customization options.  Customizable Questionnaires: OpenCATS, an applicant tracking system (ATS), provides advanced questionnaire customization capabilities, allowing administrators to tailor the application forms for each job posting.  Export to Excel: Matomo, an analytics and reporting tool, offers data export functionalities, including exporting applicant information and their answers into Excel files.  Live ATS & Statistics: OpenCATS stands out in this category as it is specifically designed for efficient applicant management and provides real-time statistics and reporting capabilities.  Quick Accept/Reject & Automated Email Communication: Mautic, an email automation tool, can be integrated into the system to enable quick accept/reject actions for applicants, along with automated email communication.  Document Management: OpenKM, a feature-rich document management system, can effectively handle document uploads, storage, and management within applicant profiles.  Automatic PDF Generation: While there are no specific scripts mentioned for this feature, it can be implemented through custom development using PHP libraries such as TCPDF or FPDF.  Multilingual Support: All the mentioned scripts, Jobberbase, OpenCATS, Matomo, Mautic, and OpenKM, can be customized to support multiple languages, including German, to cater to a diverse user base.  We kindly request experienced freelancers to provide us with a detailed quote for the estimated required hours, total cost, and project duration based on the provided requirements. Please consider the customization, integration, multilingual support, and the additional requirement of automatic PDF generation for published jobs.  We look forward to reviewing your proposals and selecting a skilled freelancer to collaborate on this project. If you have any further questions or require additional information, please do not hesitate to reach out to us.

job_description: Title: Experienced Freelancer Needed for CMOS Simulation Website Development  Description:  We are seeking a skilled and experienced freelancer to develop a CMOS simulation website. This project requires expertise in web development, specifically in building interactive simulation platforms for CMOS (Complementary Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor) circuits.  Project Overview: The primary objective of this project is to create a user-friendly website that allows users to simulate CMOS circuits and analyze their behavior. The website should provide an intuitive interface for users to design CMOS circuits, specify input signals, run simulations, and visualize the output waveforms.  Key Features and Requirements:  Circuit Design Interface: Develop a user-friendly interface where users can design CMOS circuits by selecting and placing components such as transistors, resistors, capacitors, and interconnections. The interface should support drag-and-drop functionality and provide various component options.(not mandatory)  Simulation Engine: Implement a robust simulation engine that accurately models CMOS circuit behavior based on user-designed circuits. The engine should support transient analysis, DC analysis, and AC analysis, allowing users to analyze circuit performance under different conditions.  Input Signal Generation: Provide options for users to specify input signals for their CMOS circuits. This could include the ability to define voltage sources, clock signals, and other input waveforms.  Output Visualization: Present simulation results in an intuitive and visually appealing manner. This may include generating waveform plots, voltage/current distribution maps, and other relevant visualizations.  User Accounts and Collaboration: Incorporate user account management functionality, allowing users to save and load their circuit designs, collaborate with others, and share their simulations.  Performance and Scalability: Ensure the website can handle multiple users simultaneously and deliver responsive performance even when dealing with complex circuit simulations.  Documentation and Support: Provide clear documentation and user guides to assist users in navigating the website and utilizing the simulation capabilities. Additionally, offer technical support to address any potential issues or inquiries.  Requirements for the Freelancer:  Strong proficiency in web development technologies such as HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and relevant frameworks (e.g., React, Angular, or Vue.js). Experience in developing interactive simulation platforms or similar web applications. Familiarity with CMOS circuit design and simulation principles. Solid understanding of data structures and algorithms. Ability to deliver clean and well-documented code. Good communication skills and a proactive approach to problem-solving. If you are a highly skilled freelancer who can bring this CMOS simulation website to life, we invite you to submit your proposal. Please include relevant examples of your previous work, especially projects related to web-based circuit simulation. We are looking for a committed professional who can deliver a high-quality product within the specified timeframe.  Note: Kindly note that this project requires expertise in CMOS circuit simulation and web development. If you do not have experience in both domains, we kindly request that you refrain from submitting a proposal.  We look forward to reviewing your proposals and collaborating with a talented freelancer to create an exceptional CMOS simulation website.

job_title: AI-based mental health and emotional well-being, counseling app
job_description: An app that employs AI to provide mental health support and emotional well-being. It could offer personalized recommendations for stress management, mindfulness exercises, and virtual therapy sessions based on user needs and preferences.  One challenging app idea that could utilize OpenAI is an AI-powered virtual therapist or counselor. Here's an outline of how it could work:  Natural Language Processing: The app would leverage OpenAI's language models to understand and analyze user input, including text and voice, to extract meaningful insights and understand the user's emotional state.  Sentiment Analysis: The app would employ sentiment analysis techniques to detect and analyze the user's emotions, helping identify patterns, trends, and potential areas of concern. This would involve understanding the emotional context behind the user's words and responses.  Personalized Therapy Sessions: Based on the user's input and emotional analysis, the app would generate personalized therapy sessions or counseling sessions tailored to the user's needs. It could provide guidance, coping strategies, and evidence-based techniques to address specific emotional challenges or mental health issues.  Conversational AI: OpenAI's language models would enable the app to engage in natural and empathetic conversations with users, offering a supportive and understanding environment. The AI would respond to the user's concerns, ask relevant questions, and provide appropriate feedback and guidance.  Mental Health Assessment: The app could incorporate assessment tools and questionnaires to evaluate the user's mental health status. OpenAI could assist in analyzing the responses and generating a comprehensive mental health assessment report, highlighting potential areas of concern or specific symptoms.  Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) Techniques: The app could integrate CBT techniques, leveraging OpenAI's language models to provide cognitive restructuring exercises, thought challenging exercises, and behavior change strategies. The AI could guide users through these techniques, reinforcing positive thoughts and behaviors.  Progress Tracking and Insights: The app would track user progress over time, collecting data on emotional patterns, coping strategies, and self-reported outcomes. OpenAI could assist in analyzing this data to provide insights and feedback on progress, helping users monitor their emotional well-being.  Privacy and Data Security: Given the sensitive nature of mental health information, the app would prioritize privacy and data security. Robust measures would be implemented to protect user data, comply with regulations, and ensure confidentiality.  Developing an AI-powered virtual therapist app is a complex task that requires expertise in AI, natural language processing, mental health, and therapy techniques. Ethical considerations should be taken into account, and the app should not be seen as a substitute for professional mental health care but rather as a supportive tool to complement therapy. Collaboration with mental health professionals would be crucial to ensure the app's effectiveness and safety. job_title: Excel VBA Based Macro development for Inventory Analysis System
job_description: I am looking for an Excel VBA expert to develop a macro for an inventory analysis system. The project requires the analysis of opening stock, sales, purchase, stock transfer and closing stock data.  The output needed is a table with the SKU, Number of Days it had 0 Stock, Movement of Inventory between those dates.  The ideal candidate should have experience in Excel VBA and inventory analysis.  Specific requirements for the project include:  - Analysis of the attached sample sheet to create an output table in a separate worksheet - Ability to handle the following scenarios -- SKU was in stock, sold out, then came back in stock and sold -- SKU was purchased for the first time in the window hence has no opening stock entry -- Macro should be able to handle different numbers of rows in the Input sheet as it is an export from an accounting system.  SKUs that can be checked for the above mentioned conditions in the attached sheet are - 3021732 - 3021733 - 3021736 - 3041984  Clarification of definitions - Days it has 0 stock - If the date range provided is 1st April, 2023 to 15th April, 2023 and the product was in stock on the 1st of April, sold out on the 3rd of April and came back in stock on the 10th of April the total Days it has 0 stock is 3rd to 10th = 7 Days. Continuing the example if it sold out again on the 12th of April totally it would be out of stock for 7 + 2 Days = 9 Days - Movement = Total Sales / Number of Days it was in stock. So from the earlier example the total number of days in stock would be 6 days - 1st, 2nd and 3rd plus 10th, 11th and 12th.  The project will require attention to detail and strong problem-solving skills. The ideal candidate should be able to work independently and meet project deadlines.  Please go through the attached sheet properly to understand the scope of the project and ask any questions as needed. Without going through the sheet it will be impossible to say whether you can do this project.

Preface: I started to make a simple Python script for my professional practice. It is intended to be used by myself, and nobody else. It is intended to be used in a medical practice to improve the robustness, and remove some uncertainty, of a medical treatment administered within a niche modality. It is not intended for retail sale (no FDA clearance required).  Purpose: The purpose of this software is to visualize, and analyze, the planned positions of a medical linear accelerator in relation to the external surface of a patient receiving radiation therapy.  Design: The software should prompt the user for the files necessary to perform this analysis--an RTStruct DICOM file containing a contour named 'Body', and a .csv file containing the planned (x, y, z) coordinates of the planned source and target points of the medical accelerator, from which the exact position and orientation of the medical accelerator can be determined (except one degree of freedom, the determination of which is outside the scope of this project).  As a minimally acceptable piece of software, the script should display, in 3D, the patient's external surface, the central axis of the linear accelerator (a line), and a rough model (two cylinders) of the accelerator at its planned position. The user should be able to click left/right buttons to cycle through the various planned positions of the accelerator (again, positions are defined by source-target coordinates provided by the user in a .csv file). The 3D visualization should be user interactive, allowing the user to rotate, pan, and zoom on the 3D models. It doesn't need to look pretty, it just needs to work.  As a maximally developed piece of software, the script would also sort the positions of the linear accelerator to match the order in which they are planned to be used for patient treatment. The script would also determine, for each planned position, the shortest distance between the surface of the modeled linear accelerator and the surface of the patient model. It would also generate a simple .pdf report of this analysis (performed for all planned positions), with some graphics.  A sample set of RTStruct DICOM file (mannequin model, not a real person) and .csv file would be provided. Specifications of the dimensions of the medical accelerator would also be provided (length, radius, etc.)  As mentioned previously in this description, this project is already started. It uses PyQt library for user interface, PyDicom and numpy to extract patient body surface contour information, VTK to display body contour and modeled medical accelerator. I could probably finish this project on my own (with the help of ChatGPT), but now I want to get a quote to see how much it would cost to expedite the development of this project by a professional. I don't expect it would require more than 10 hours for a professional to complete this... probably not more than 5 hours. Maybe less than 2 hours. Again, this is a simple script, with a very, very narrow use-case.  Willing to pay in Bitcoin (BTC).

Authentication PubMighty authentication system to ensure secure access for users that include:  a) Login: Users can log in to their PubMighty accounts using their credentials. b) Sign Up: New users can create an account on PubMighty. c) Reset Password: Users can reset their passwords in case they forget or want to change them. d) Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) System: Provides an additional layer of security for user accounts.  5. Publisher Dashboard The Publisher Dashboard serves as a central hub for publishers to manage their PubMighty products and access various features. It includes the following sections:  a) Global Dashboard: Provides an overview of all PubMighty products and their performance. b) PubMighty Links: A product-specific dashboard that allows publishers to manage their shortened links, create new ones, and customize settings. c) Publisher Reports: Offers comprehensive reports and analytics on the performance of publishers' content and links. d) Publisher Referral: This enables publishers to track and manage referrals to the PubMighty platform. e) Publisher Tools/Developer API Tools: Provides web tools and settings for developers to integrate PubMighty functionalities into their own websites or applications. f) Publisher Payouts: Allows publishers to view and manage their payout details. g) Publisher Transactions: Provides a record of all transactions related to publishers' activities on the platform. h) Publisher Settings: Allows publishers to manage their profile, billing information, payout preferences, and security settings.  6. Admin Dashboard The Admin Dashboard serves as the control panel for PubMighty administrators. It includes the following sections:  a) PubMighty Links: This enables administrators to manage all links on the platform, including settings and configurations. b) Admin Reports: Offers comprehensive reports and analytics on the overall performance of PubMighty. c) Advertising Rates: Allows administrators to define and manage advertising rates based on different types of advertising. d) Admin Manage Users: Provides features to view, edit, and manage user accounts, including session management and administrative login controls. e) Admin Manage Payouts: Enables administrators to manage payout-related activities, including listing and bulk exporting. f) Admin Settings: Provides options to configure general settings, email notifications, security measures, payout settings, advertising settings, revenue management, and other tools.  7. Reports and Filtering  PubMighty includes robust reporting capabilities for both publishers and administrators. Users can filter reports based on various dimensions using the data available in the database, allowing for in-depth analysis and insights.  8. Project Objective  The primary objective of PubMighty is to empower publishers by providing them with tools and resources to effectively monetize their content and maximize their revenue potential.

The project involves implementing specific enhancements, including aging accounts receivable, collection reporting, inventory reporting, additional metrics module, centralized multi-store dashboard, and refining item destination handling within the accounting and balance sheet functionalities.  Project Details:  Compatible Browsers: IE11, Firefox, Safari, Opera, Chrome Files Included: JavaScript JS, HTML, CSS, PHP Software Framework: Laravel Software Version: PHP 9.x Project Scope:  Aging - Accounts Receivable:  Separate accounts receivable based on aging periods (e.g., 30, 60, 90 days). Implement functionalities to track and manage outstanding invoices for improved financial analysis. RRR (Collection Report):  Develop a collection report to track payments that need to be collected. Separate pending collections to facilitate efficient follow-up. Inventory Report:  Implement an inventory reporting module to track and manage pest control products and supplies. Provide insights into inventory levels, stock movement, and reordering needs. Additional Metrics Module:  Develop a metrics module to calculate and display key performance indicators (KPIs) for the pest control business. Metrics can include items, units, number of services, sales per service, and total sales. Calculate total sales by multiplying the number of services by the sales per service. Dashboard for Centralization and Multi-Store:  Create a centralized dashboard to provide a comprehensive view of multiple store locations or companies within the pest control business. Include features such as sales data, financial summaries, inventory status, and performance metrics. Enable seamless navigation between different stores or locations for efficient management. Item Destination Handling in Accounting and Balance Sheet:  Refine the item destination handling within the accounting module. Ensure accurate allocation of items to their respective destinations, particularly in the balance sheet. Implement proper accounting entries and reporting for item movements, ensuring they reflect the correct destination. Requirements:  Proven experience as a Laravel developer with expertise in modifying existing systems. Strong proficiency in PHP development and Laravel framework. In-depth knowledge of accounting principles and processes, including balance sheet handling and item destination tracking. Experience in developing accounting systems with accurate item allocation and reporting. Familiarity with inventory management principles and their integration with accounting systems. Proficiency in JavaScript, HTML, CSS, and PHP. Solid understanding of databases, particularly MySQL. Experience in creating interactive dashboards and centralized systems. Excellent problem-solving skills and meticulous attention to detail. Effective communication and collaboration abilities. If you are a skilled Laravel developer with expertise in accounting and experience in modifying systems, we invite you to join our project. Please provide examples of your previous work, particularly showcasing your expertise in implementing accounting enhancements and refining item destination handling within accounting and balance sheet functionalities. We look forward to discussing the project further and collaborating to enhance our pest control billing and service management system with the requested modifications.

Address any issues, bugs, or feature requests that arise during this period promptly.  Note: The above requirements outline the key aspects of the project. A) Database design: Determine the structure of your database based on the information you want to store. Identify the necessary tables and the relationships between them. For example, you might have a table for suppliers, one for customers, one for documents (invoices, orders), one for products, and so on. Define the attributes of each table and the primary and foreign keys to establish the relationships between them.  B) Database creation: Use a relational database management system (RDBMS) such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, or Microsoft SQL Server to create your database. Follow the specific instructions of the chosen RDBMS to create the tables, define the attributes and relationships.  C) Data extraction: Use the information extraction techniques described earlier to extract the relevant data from the documents. You can use machine learning methods or other techniques to extract the desired information and associate it with the corresponding database tables.  D) Data insertion into the database: Use SQL language or a data import tool provided by your RDBMS to insert the extracted data into the database. Use INSERT statements to insert records into the appropriate tables, following the defined relationships.  E) Quality control: Verify the correctness of the data inserted into the database. Run queries to extract the data and compare it with the original documents to ensure the accuracy of the extracted information.  F) Data querying and analysis: Use SQL language to query the database and retrieve data based on specific criteria. You can run complex queries to extract detailed information, generate reports, or analyze the data for decision-making purposes.  G) Updating and maintenance: If the Document AI project is ongoing and new documents are being extracted regularly, you will need to plan for updating the database with the new data. The purpose of this project is to develop a social media intelligence platform that leverages artificial intelligence (AI) to collect, analyze, and derive valuable insights from social media data. The platform aims to assist businesses, organizations, and individuals in understanding social media trends, sentiments, and conversations, enabling them to make informed decisions, improve their strategies, and enhance their online presence.  Objectives: Develop a robust social media intelligence platform capable of aggregating data from multiple social media platforms.  Apply AI techniques to extract meaningful insights, sentiments, and trends from the collected data.  Provide real-time monitoring and analysis of social media activities, including mentions, hashtags, user engagement, and sentiment analysis.  Enable users to track and compare their brand's performance against competitors on social media.  Generate customizable reports and visualizations to present data in a clear and actionable manner.  Implement advanced features such as influencer identification, social listening, and sentiment analysis at scale.  Ensure data privacy, security, and compliance with relevant regulations.  Key Features: Data Collection: Develop data collection mechanisms to gather information from popular social media platforms, including but not limited to Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, LinkedIn, and YouTube.  Sentiment Analysis: Utilize natural language processing (NLP) techniques to analyze text and determine sentiment, allowing users to understand the overall tone of social media conversations.  Trend Detection: Apply AI algorithms to identify emerging trends, popular topics, and hashtags to help users stay up-to-date with the latest discussions.  Competitor Analysis: Enable users to compare their social media performance against competitors, benchmarking key metrics such as engagement, reach, and sentiment.  Real-time Monitoring: Implement real-time monitoring capabilities to provide users with immediate alerts and notifications regarding relevant mentions, trends, or changes in sentiment.  Reporting and Visualization: Generate comprehensive reports and visually appealing dashboards to present insights, enabling users to understand and share data effectively.  Suggested Technology Stack: Programming Languages: Python, JavaScript Frameworks and Libraries: TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, NLTK, SpaCy, Django, Flask, React, Vue.js Cloud Services: Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud Platform (GCP), Microsoft Azure Database Management: MongoDB, MySQL, PostgreSQL

Leveraging this system, fraudsters attempt to gain access to such services by identity theft or by creating a fictional individual. Once access to a new bank account is granted, the fraudster quickly takes advantage of the accompanying line of credit or uses the account to receive illicit payments. In this scenario, where the fraudster succeeds, the bank sustains all costs as there is no way of tracing the fraudster's true identity.  Using a dataset gathered from online bank account opening applications, the goal of this practical assignment is to provide informed and well-documented advice on which predictive modelling solution to use to detect fraudulent applications accurately.  Each instance (row) of the dataset represents an application described by 32 features. The label of each instance is stored in the"is_fraud" target variable. A full description of the features that compose the dataset is available here.    Tasks Using the [login to view URL] data set, you have a set of main tasks to accomplish as described next. Still, you are free to include other tasks to increase the value of your assignment.   Task 1: Data Understanding and Preparation and Descriptive Analytics This task involves summarizing and visualizing the data to provide valuable insights. Consider questions that could be interesting to check with the available data and provide answers using textual summaries or data visualization. Based on this analysis, you should also check if it is necessary to carry out any data clean-up and pre-processing steps. Additionally, you can apply clustering techniques if you find them helpful to provide valuable insights.    Task 2: Predictive Modelling From the available data, you should define the data set used for the classification task at hand. Different models should be considered, and the choice of the final model should be justified.     Task 3: Kaggle Competition Additionally, you should submit your solution for the [login to view URL] data set to the Kaggle Competition.  Note that the goal is not to overfit the test set data to achieve a good position in the public leaderboard. Your rank position in the private leaderboard will be accounted for in the final grade.    Tools You can use R or Python. You can find material for dynamic reporting in R with markdown if you choose to use R. Deliverables file containing the following items:  the source of two ready-to-execute dynamic reports or notebooks with all the code necessary to reproduce the results you present for i) Task 1; and ii) Task 2, including the solution submitted to Kaggle - Task 3, i.e. it should be possible to replicate your predictions; any complementary files needed to execute your report (e.g. data files, data objects) should be submitted as well.

job_title: Build a Database of Real Estate Market Cap Rates in The US
job_description: We're looking for someone to help create a database of market cap rates for small apartment complexes in every Zip Code, County, Metro/Micro Area, and State throughout the US.  The database will have current market cap rate estimations in each market as well as historical market cap rates ideally going back to 2010.  Quick explanation of cap rate:  Cap rate is determined by dividing net operating income (NOI) by purchase price. NOI is determined by subtracting expenses from gross rental income.  Operating expenses used to calculate NOI are as follows: 1. Property taxes 2. Insurance 3. Vacancy loss 4. Repairs and maintenance 5. Landscaping/ snow removal 6. Trash removal 7. Owner share of utilities 8. Misc legal/ accounting  Management is another expense used for large apartment complexes, but for small apartments we'll typically not include property management.  If you're reading this say "Water Buffalo" in your message.  Once NOI is determined, market value (price) is calculated by dividing the NOI by the market cap rate. The market cap rate is the average yield an investor would expect to receive by buying a similar asset in that particular market.  This is the same concept as buying a bond for the market-rate amount of yield it will produce.  Example: A 5 unit apartment building has a net operating income of $50k. If the market cap rate is 5% then the market value (price) would be $1M. ($50,000 / .05 = $1,000,000)  A market cap rate is determined by comparing the recent sales of similar assets. This is the same concept as how a car's value is determined by the recent sale of a similar car. Or a phone's value is determined by the recent sale of a similar phone.  In order to accomplish this we'll need to: 1. Collect median price for apartments in a given market. 2. Collect median gross rent for the same sized apartment in a given market. 3. Collect all median operating expenses in a given market OR find a data source that provides an aggregate total of all relevant expenses. 4. Create a database that maintains historical and current cap rates by market.  Data sources we can use include: Census ACS, BLS, HUD, and other publicly available sources. Zillow and Redfin also have some housing data that could be useful.  We'll need to think outside of the box and get creative since oftentimes the data we'll find needs to be adjusted to fit our criteria.  An example of this would be calculating expenses by solving for owner-provided property expenses given in the ACS, solving for an subtracting tenant-paid utilities from this amount, then correcting for the appropriate tax rate that would be paid after a market value sale.  The result of this should be an accurate monthly expense amount which we can subtract from median rent to determine NOI and ultimately the market cap rate.  I have other ideas which we can brainstorm on, I've already collected most of the relevant publicly available data in all markets.  An ideal candidate has a base level understand of data analysis and real estate market analysis.   Let me know if you're up for the challenge. I look forward to meeting you!

Martin wants to open a copy shop.  He has asked you to develop a profit model for him and to write a brief report on what your profit model indicates that he should do.  Specifically, he wants to know:  Under the assumptions he has given you (see below), what is his profit per year? Under the assumptions he has given you, what is his breakeven demand per day? He wonders what his profitability would look like if demand were lower or higher than his base case.  He thinks that realistically, demand could be anywhere from 1000 copies a day to 2250 copies a day.  He'd like to know his profitability at each level, in increments of 250 copies per day. The copy machine company representative provided him with a probability distribution of daily demand, based on other stores that are in areas similar to Martin's.  Using those probability distributions, and the demand sensitivity analysis, what would be his expected annual profit? At what level of demand would it be profitable to lease a second copier?  A second copier would not change the level of demand.  It would be leased at the same rate as the first copier.  The shop's fixed costs would stay the same. What would you advise him to do based on what you have learned from your model? Prepare a spreadsheet model and a brief report to Cousin Martin.  Here is the information he has provided:  Annual rental for a big copy machine:    $40,000 Fixed costs for leasing and running his store, monthly:  $500 Variable costs per copy:  $0.03 Revenue per copy:  $0.12 He plans to open the shop 300 days/year.  However, he could open more days if he needed to.  He believes that opening more days would not affect the demand per day. He thinks a base case for demand will be 1,500 copies each day the store opens.  However, he recognizes that this is a guess at this point. He thinks he should start with one copier.  The copier has the capacity to produce 2250 copies per day.  If demand is higher than this, he will have to either turn away business or lease a second copier. The copy company salesperson supplied him with the following probability distribution for the number of copies demanded based on stores in locations similar to his: Copies per Day	probability 1000	5% 1250	10% 1500	25% 1750	35% 2000	20% 2250	5%

job_title: MPEG custom encoder/decoder with visual tests (bit level manipulation)
job_description: Our client wants has an older broadcast appliance that needs to have realtime encryption tested and validated.   We basically want to encrypt the video on the encoded frame level, ie (I, P, B frames) we want to bit manipulate frames before they go into the Huffman encoded stream. A custom encoding will be applied to the frames then they go to Huffman and the reverse on the decoder. Import the file and encode the frames using Mpeg Inside the Mpeg decoder, we should isolate the packets and isolate frames of the three types we should then encrypt the frames with the encryption function then we should have the encrypted data repackaged the MPEG encoder should finish the job and continue with the process of writing them to the file The decryption should follow the same approach; then put it in reverse.  Extras: The encoding key will be a quadratic chaotic map (this is very easy the achieve once we have an entry into the raw encoded frames before Huffman) basically the map is the key that we shift bits according to so no worries there.  Simulation program for security analysis and output analysis 1)Specification of the tested frames 2) a) Histogram analysis b) pixel distribution for the three RGB channels of the evaluated frame (for the original frame and encrypted frame) 3) Correlation coefficient analysis between plain and encrypted frames. 4)Entropy analysis 5)Peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) 6) Number of pixel change rate (NPCR) 7)Unified average changing intensity (UACI) & MSE 8) Structural similarity (SSIM) index 9)Differential measurement using different control parameter measuring (MSE, UACI, NPCR)& Differential measurement using different initial condition measuring ((MSE, UACI,NPCR) 10)Encryption speed analysis   Will provide the key map generation function, and appropriately ranged seed values for the contributor, and a paper describing the attacks.  Deliverables  Encoder: Inputs: - Seeds for key generation - A video file - may add as well the encryption function acting on the bits (may be hard coded) Output: - an MPEG encoded file with the desired encryptions - the file should be playable on any MPEG player - The video playable is supposed to be in intelligible  Decoder: Inputs: - Seeds for key generation - Mpeg encoded file (the encrypted file) - may add as well the encryption function acting on the bits (may be hard coded) Output: - A video file - should be playable by a video player - supposed to be resembling the original video   Visualization Generator Inputs - first video file - second video file Outputs - the outputs and visualizations listed above  Attack script Inputs - A video file - attack type and intensity ( eg salt and pepper attack) Output - A video file of the same format with the desired attack applied

job_title: Google Scholar scrape and analysis program
job_description: I'm looking to hire a freelancer to develop an automated program that collects and analyzes data from Google Scholar. The program needs to be written in either Python, C++, or JavaScript, and must be able to generate spreadsheet outputs. The program should be manually initiated whenever an analysis is required, as opposed to running on a regular schedule.  This will be a Windows-compatible desktop program to receive as input a Google Scholar search string and output a table of the year-by-year citation history of the top ten most frequently cited documents for each year.  There are two relevant levels of data for a Google Scholar search. The primary level is the initial search results which include each document found by the search, name of the document, number times the document has been cited in other documents, the year of publication, and a link to the secondary level. The secondary level is the "Cites" for each document, which include the name and year of publication of those secondary/citing documents.  The program needs to identify the ten most frequently cited documents in each year for the search string ("Top Documents") - so will need to count the Cites per year for each primary document. Then for each Top Document report the number of citations for each year since it was published (ie before and after the year that qualified them as a Top Author). The output should be a .xlsx spreadsheet (or otherwise Excel import compatible) showing the list of all Top Documents and their number of cites per year.  Deliverables will be a working executable file for the program that will work on Windows 11 Home; source code; and a brief written description of the process.

job_title: Supervised and GAN-based Techniques for Increasing  Fine Texture Details in Super-Resolution
job_description: Significance: Super-resolution is an essential task in many areas, such as computer vision, remote sensing, and medical imaging. For accurate analysis and decision-making, it is crucial to be able to recover high-quality images from low-resolution inputs. Recovery of fine texture details when super- resolving at high upscaling factors is still difficult, despite significant advancements in the field. This project is significant because it will create a new method for super-resolution that can successfully recover these details and enhance the output's perceptual quality. These details are lost in conventional techniques based on interpolation or deconvolution. Deep learning techniques have recently gained popularity, and in particular supervised and unsupervised GAN-based methods have shown promise in producing photorealistic textures from severely downscaled images. To enhance perceptual quality and get around the drawbacks of current approaches, more research is necessary. Proposed Solution: To improve fine texture details in super-resolution, I offer a two-part solution in this project. In the first section, I'll create a supervised deep learning method that makes use of various loss functions to enhance the perceptual quality of the images that are generated. I will specifically look at the use of adversarial loss, which motivates the generator to create photorealistic images, and perceptual loss, which employs a pre-trained deep network to measure the similarity between the generated and ground truth images in terms of perceptual features. To determine the advantages and disadvantages of each approach, I will also contrast model-based approaches (such as bicubic interpolation) with model-free approaches (such as deep learning). The proposed solution is to create a new deep learning model for super-resolution that is capable of recovering fine texture details when upscaling by large factors. In a supervised setting, the model will be trained with multiple loss functions, including mean squared error (MSE) loss, perceptual loss, and an adversarial loss. I will extend the supervised approach to the GAN setting in the second part by incorporating adversarial training. To address issues such as mode collapse and instability, I will investigate various GAN architectures and loss functions. I will also investigate how perceptual loss can be used in the GAN setting to improve the perceptual quality of the generated images. To test and train our proposed solution, I will use publicly available datasets such as DIV2K and Set5.

I teach a seminar where students make short presentations in front of the class and everybody rates the presentation on a standardized excel-form (see attachment). The rating is done in freeform text and on a 1-5 scale (-- - 0 + ++) for several categories, such as media use or rhetoric. Students fill out one of these forms for every presentation. So when class-size is 20, there are 20x(20-1) = 380 forms. What I need to extract from these forms is: - How good was the presentation (compared to all others) - How good a critic is each student (compared to all others)  Therefore, I need an R-Script (or several) that does the following things:  - Ask for Basic Information: What was the Name of the Seminar, what Semester is it, what is the Import-Folder, what is the Output-Folder, where is the file with Meta-Information about all presentations?  -Import the Meta-Information from a csv-file. In this file, every student (and thus presentation) is assigned a number. Through this number, every excel-form is uniquely connected to a presenter and a reviewer, as every student puts his/her number in as the reviewer and the number of the presenter.  -Import all Excel-Files in the Import-Directory into a Dataframe. Only relevant cells should be kept! Convert the x on the categorical scale (-- - 0 + ++) to the corresponding value on a numeric scale centered on 0 (-2 -1 0 1 2). One row of the data frame should correspond to one excel-File with one column per relevant cell.  - For each student, a PDF report is created (see attachment). The first page contains Meta-Information about the presentation, coming from the Meta-File. Additional scores are displayed, showing how many forms the student filled out and to what degree those forms were complete (%). The second page contains 8 Histogram-Plots, using the numeric feedback on the students own presentation. Each Histogram should contain not only the students presentation result, but also the mean result across all presentations as a reference. Additionally, each plot contains a vertical line for the average rating of the students presentation and all presentations. Page three is built in the same way as page two, but all the ratings the student has given are the basis of the plots, instead of the ratings the student has received. The last pages contain two tables with free-text feedback. The first with feedback for the students presentation, sorted by category. The second with feedback the student has given, sorted in the same way. The whole PDF should have some general formatting with page-numbering, a logo on each page, and the name of the presenter/the seminar.  - Export a csv file with the Meta-Information, but add the average numerical feedback scores for each student and how many forms (and to what degree) each student has filled out.

job_title: Short Time Forecast for Crytocurrancies Pairs (Up to 11 minutes)
job_description: Project Overview:  I am seeking an experienced data analyst to create a short time forecast for cryptocurrency pairs, with a maximum time frame of 11 minutes. The ideal candidate will have experience in technical analysis, machine learning, and hybrid approaches to forecasting.  Forecasting Method:  I am open to all forecasting methods, but accuracy is my top priority. The chosen method should provide the most accurate results possible.  Output Format:  I prefer raw data as the output format, as I will be analyzing the data further on my own. No need for charts or written reports.  Timeline:  I need this project completed as soon as possible. Time is of the essence, so please only apply if you have availability to work on this project immediately.  My Project Details:  To provide the forecast value at any given moment in the next 300, 360, 480, 540, 600 & 660 seconds of any cryptocurrency pair at any given moment.  The list of the 31 Symbol pairs of interest is attached.  In addition of providing the 6 forecast at any given moment, provide the accuracy of each of the 6.  For example:  Request time 20:00 Value BTC-USD at 20:00: 30,245.57  Forecasts (Your data output): 300s	- - - - -	20:05	- - - - -	30,262.37	- - - - -	Above	- - - - -	88% 360s	- - - - -	20:06	- - - - -	30,241.36	- - - - -	Below	- - - - -	64% 480s	- - - - -	20:08	- - - - -	30,264.37	- - - - -	Above	- - - - -	78% 540s	- - - - -	20:09	- - - - -	30,268.83	- - - - -	Above	- - - - -	69% 600s	- - - - -	20:10	- - - - -	30,242.14	- - - - -	Below	- - - - -	71% 660s	- - - - -	20:11	- - - - -	30,262.95	- - - - -	Above	- - - - -	87% *You may NOT include the second column nor the "-" signs  IMPORTANT NOTE: THE EXACT VALUE IS NOT THAT IMPORTANT, BUT, IF THE VALUE AT ANY FORECAST TIME WILL BE ABOVE OR BELOW VS. THE VALUE AT REQUEST TIME.  Considering this note, please provide the accuracy you will have OVERALL, not predicting exact values but predicting if the forecast value will be above or below Vs. the value at request time?  Your data output will be:  a) A table as previous one per symbol pair.  b) A single list with all 180 forecasts (6 forecast times 31 symbol pairs), separated by time bracket (all 300s, all 360s, etc) in the same table format as before, sorted, each group, descending per accuracy percentage.  c) A single list with all 180 forecasts (6 forecast times 31 symbol pairs), in the same table format as before, sorted descending per accuracy percentage.  Once the solution is done, I will need 5 working days to test it and measure your accuracy.  I will check for each pair all 6 forecast. Times 31 pairs, I will perform 186 test per occurrence. For 5 working days, that's a total of 930 tests. If your accuracy is as promised, I will release the Milestone.  IMPORTANT:To consider it a valid forecast, I WILL NOT MEASURE the actual forecast values, just if the forecast was in the right direction, above or below Vs. the value at request time.

job_title: Hyperparameter and structural time series component selection optimisation to ensure model convergence
job_description: I need a freelancer to optimize the hyperparameters and structural time series component selection of my model to ensure convergence whilst maintaining model accuracy. The project requires expertise in Python programming language. I have a specific dataset in mind that will be used for the project. Ideal skills and experience for this job include:  - Strong background in machine learning and data analysis - Proficiency in Python programming language - Experience with hyperparameter optimization and time series analysis - Familiarity with structural time series models - Ability to work with large datasets and optimize computational time  Requirements Specification:  Objective:  Develop a time series forecasting model using TensorFlow Probability Structural Time Series (TFP STS) to predict future values of a given dataset.  Requirements:  1/. Loss Function Monitoring - Implement a mechanism to track the training loss during model optimization. - Ensure that the loss function decreases over time and stabilizes, indicating convergence. - Include periodic logging and visualization of the training loss.  2/. Trace Plots: - Generate trace plots for all model parameters during Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) sampling. - Verify that the traces are stationary and do not exhibit obvious trends, indicative of convergence. - Include functionality to visualize and save trace plots for further analysis.  3/. Autocorrelation Plots: - Create autocorrelation plots for MCMC samples of each model parameter. - Confirm that autocorrelation is low, suggesting that the chain has mixed well and converged. - Implement a feature to visualize and save autocorrelation plots for review.  4/. Effective Sample Size (ESS): - Calculate the effective sample size for each model parameter. - Ensure that ESS values are sufficiently large, indicating better convergence. - Provide a summary of ESS values for all parameters and include functionality to export these values.  5/. R-hat (Gelman-Rubin Diagnostic): - Compute the R-hat statistic for each model parameter. - Verify that R-hat values are close to 1, signaling good convergence. - Implement a summary report of R-hat values for all parameters and include an export feature.  6/. Posterior Predictive Checks: - Perform posterior predictive checks by generating simulated data from the fitted model. - Compare simulated data to the observed data in terms of summary statistics and overall structure. - Ensure that the model demonstrates satisfactory convergence by producing realistic simulations. - Include functionality to visualize and save posterior predictive check results.  7/. Widely Applicable Information Criterion (WAIC) and Deviance Information Criterion (DIC): - Calculate the WAIC and DIC for the fitted Bayesian model. - Ensure that the WAIC and DIC values are reasonably low, indicating a good balance between model fit and complexity. - Track the WAIC and DIC values during model selection, comparing different model configurations to choose the best one. - Provide a summary report of WAIC and DIC values for different models and include functionality to export these values.  Deliverables:  - A well-documented Jupyter Notebook or Python script demonstrating the entire model development process. - A summary report that includes the convergence diagnostic results (trace plots, autocorrelation plots, ESS, R-hat values, and posterior predictive checks). - A serialized version of the trained model for future use. - A user guide detailing the usage of the provided code and explanation of the convergence diagnostics.  Note: The notebook already contains many of these metrics including MAPE (posterior predictive checks), R-hat and a traceplot. Please ensure these metrics are shown for each parameter. In other words, please ensure that the R-hat, traceplot, ESS, WAIC and DIC shows values for each parameter.

job_title: If you need to hire someone for Excel data entry work, there are several things you should consider to ensure you find the right person for the job.
job_description: If you need to hire someone for Excel data entry work, there are several things you should consider to ensure you find the right person for the job.  Here are some tips for hiring someone for Excel data entry work:  Define the scope of the project: Before you start looking for candidates, it's important to define the scope of the project. This includes the type of data you need entered, the volume of data, and the timeframe for completion.  Identify the required skills: Excel data entry work requires a strong understanding of Excel and basic data entry skills. Depending on the complexity of the data, you may also need someone with experience in data analysis, data management, or other related skills.  Create a clear job description: To attract the right candidates, you need to create a clear job description that outlines the requirements of the position, including the skills and experience required, the expected deliverables, and the timeline for completion.  Post the job on relevant platforms: There are several online platforms where you can post job listings for data entry work, including freelancer marketplaces, job boards, and social media platforms. Be sure to post the job on platforms that are relevant to your industry and target audience.  Review resumes and portfolios: Once you start receiving applications, review resumes and portfolios to evaluate candidates' experience and qualifications. Look for candidates who have relevant experience and a strong track record of completing similar projects.  Conduct interviews: After narrowing down your list of candidates, conduct interviews to assess their communication skills, work ethic, and problem-solving abilities. Ask questions related to their experience with Excel and data entry work to evaluate their proficiency.  Check references: Before making a final decision, check references to ensure the candidate has a positive track record of completing projects on time and to a high standard.  By following these tips, you can hire the right person for your Excel data entry work and ensure the project is completed efficiently and accurately.

Finally, we would need to interpret the results of the analysis and present them in a meaningful way. This could involve creating reports or presentations that summarize the findings and provide insights into the performance of the company and its products in the retail industry.  Overall, creating a complex code for financial data of an industry in a real-life scenario would require a combination of data gathering, cleaning and preprocessing, analysis, and interpretation. job_title: Phase 2. A webpage for academical experiment that allow building photo collages from images via Google Images API
job_description: Our team wants to build a webpage for experimental purposes.  We aim to see how people imagine a specific concept without using a text description. Thus, participants in the survey would use images to describe their feelings and understandings of a specific concept. For example, what do they think about a specific Eating experience in a specific country?  In specific, we can describe briefly the project below. Besides, there is a wireframe that we want to have in the end.  Front end requirement:  1. The webpage needs to have a database that stores information about users and their selections on the survey.  2. The webpage database needs to connect with two platforms API: Amazon Mturk ([login to view URL]) and Prolific ([login to view URL]).  The purpose is to make sure once participants complete the survey, and submit their Mturk or Prolific ID, these platforms will reward them accordingly.  UX flow: Please check the demo flow here along with the below description: [login to view URL]  3. Initially, when users go into the webpage for experiments, they would choose to what extent they are familiar with the concept. For example, Dining experience in Norway, Sweden, .etc.  4. After the participant's rating, we will pick which concept they are most familiar with to direct them to the relevant concept.  5. For example, if User A chooses Norwegian Dining Experience. He will be asked to use a search box to find relevant images that describe his concept or his imagination about the Norwegian Dining Experience. Relevant images here would be pulled from Google Cloud Images. The images provided by search would be limited to excluding graphics that directly point to a specific country. For example, for the Norwegian Food experience, we don't want that people can search for a Norwegian flag or something can point directly to Norway. A user will find several relevant pictures, drags them, and drops them into a white space to create a photo collage.  7. Finally, after completing the photo collage, the user will write a short description of the collage and will submit it. Frequent of the taggings in images, Top and least words, compose the short sentences based on the taggings. 4. I have a large spradsheet of data from my business.  It contains customer names and the sale price for each customer.  I need to know what the average sale price is for the company for the year.  The reason I cant do it is because some customers have multiple sales per transaction.  For example one sale may be made up of multiple parts and look like this:  Customer A = $1,000 Customer A = $500 Customer A = $2,000 Customer B = $700 Customer B = $1,200 Customer C = $3,000  CUSTOMER A:  This is one sale, but the data is made of 3 parts.  For the purpose of this research, this particular sale needs to be counted as 1 sale of $3,500, NOT 3 sales with an average of $1,166.  CUSTOMER B:  Same as customer A, this data is 1 sale of $1,900, but made of 2 parts ($700 + $1,200).  CUSTOMER C:  This is easy as its just one part and the $3,000 amount can be used to represent this sale amount.  The research needs to calculate the above scenario like this:  Customer A $3,500 + Customer B $1,900 + Customer C $3,000 = $9,400 divided by 3 customers = $3,133 average sales price across 3 customers.  There is lots of these examples in the data which makes the job too big to complete manually, so needs someone with knowledge of formulas to to this.

job_title: Legal AI platform (website, admin panel and trained AI's)
job_description: A platform that uses artificial intelligence (AI) to provide legal assistance. The platform will include the following functionalities:  Legal research: The platform will include an AI-powered legal research tool that allows users to search through large volumes of legal documents, such as case law and statutes. The tool will use natural language processing (NLP) to understand user queries and provide relevant information and guidance in response. This functionality will be particularly useful for individuals or organizations that may not have access to legal resources or expertise. Document review: The platform will include an AI-powered document review tool that can analyze legal documents, such as contracts or leases, to identify key terms and highlight any potential issues or areas of concern. The tool will use machine learning models trained on a large dataset of legal documents to accurately identify relevant terms and issues. This functionality will be helpful for individuals or organizations who want to ensure that their legal documents are accurate and complete.  Predictive analysis: The platform will include an AI-powered predictive analysis tool that can analyze past legal cases and decisions to make predictions about the likely outcomes of future cases. The tool will use machine learning models trained on a large dataset of legal cases and decisions to make predictions about the likelihood of certain outcomes. This functionality will be useful for individuals or organizations preparing for a legal dispute or trying to negotiate a settlement.  Contract drafting: The platform will include an AI-powered contract drafting tool that can generate draft contracts based on input from users. The tool will use machine learning models trained on a large dataset of legal contracts to generate drafts that are consistent with common legal terms and practices. This functionality will be helpful for individuals or organizations who want to save time and ensure that their contracts are accurate and complete. Case management: The platform will include a case management system that allows users to manage and organize their legal cases, schedule appointments and deadlines, and track the progress of their cases. The system will use a database to store and organize case information, and will provide users with alerts and reminders to help them stay on top of their legal matters.  Legal document automation: The platform will include an AI-powered tool that can automatically generate legal documents based on user input and pre-determined templates. This functionality will be useful for individuals or organizations who want to save time and reduce the risk of errors when creating legal documents. Predictive legal analytics: The platform will include an AI-powered tool that can analyze legal data and provide insights and predictions about trends and patterns in the legal industry. This functionality will be useful for individuals or organizations who want to stay informed about developments in the legal field and make informed decisions.  Legal chatbot: The platform will include a chatbot that uses natural language processing (NLP) to understand and respond to user queries about legal matters. The chatbot will be able to provide basic legal information and guidance, as well as refer users to legal professionals for more specialized assistance.  The initial training of the platform's AI models will be done by the developer using data provided by us, but please specify what you need exactly, and the models can then be further trained and fine-tuned using the platform's admin panel. No external AI's can be used.  The end product should be a complete solution, including a website, an admin portal, and a fully trained and functional AI. Including full information on how we can further train the models through the admin panel.

job_description: Project Title: Keyword Rank Analyzer  Objective: The objective of this project is to develop a cross-platform SaaS app (iOS, Android, and Web) that allows users to input a keyword and determine its potential to rank within 7 days using KGR techniques. Current: We have a PHP/mySQL database for online SAT and ACT-type testing and reporting. The database is slow, but it works.  This project is to create a new database using node js and mongo so that the system will be efficient for online testing (for exams like the SAT and ACT exams) and for reporting student results.  The system needs to work for different types of tests (like SAT, PSAT, ACT, and LSAT) and provide a user-friendly online testing system for students to use. It needs to be able to accommodate 5000 students testing simultaneously and then handle and store the data for retrieval and analysis in a robust reporting system. Students can also test using a pen-and-paper format with their data uploaded via csv.  There will be one or two system admin logins for those of us creating the tests on the backend. These admins will need access to everything.  Student registration data needs to be uploaded via csv.  In general, each test has 4 sections of 30-50 questions each. Most questions are multiple choice with 4 or 5 answer choices. Some math questions are free response. Questions (and sometimes answer choices for each question) must be randomized within the section. The quizzes are timed (each individual section has its own timer). There are also timing modifications (so, for example, some students with learning disabilities get 150% of the standard time or double time). So we need to create "types" of students that have specific time modifications associated with them.  Students will be assigned a particular test and can only take that test one time. But, we need to have the ability to reset the exam so the student can re-take it from the beginning if they need to. We also need the ability for students to re-log in and resume testing in the event that their internet goes out, power goes off, etc., while testing.  Students will need a limited login that will allow them access to their test and results.  The testing platform needs to include a dashboard that allows the teacher to have a synchronized start for all students in a group. It needs to track student time and progress so that students can resume testing in the event of an internet failure, etc. Administrators need to monitor real-time usage to see how many students are actively testing, where they are in the test, etc.  Reports analyze student performance individually and/or as a group (for example, all the students at a single school campus or multiple campuses or all students who have taken a particular exam). 2 sample reports are available from the current system. Other reports (such as, a list of students who have not yet tested, etc.) are also needed.  District administrators need to be able to access the database in view-only mode to produce student reports and group reports for their school(s). The "subadmin" users from particular clients will need access to just their client data - to produce reports but not to change anything in the database (like a read-only access).   I attached a more detailed SOW (no highlight = Phase I, blue highlight = Phase II) as well as a document that represents the flow of the current database. The current database is for information, but the new one can be completely different - whatever makes sense.  We need to move the data from the current DB into the new DB so when we run reports it includes the data collected in the current DB.

Have End of the day data of Option CE, PE Price and OI as well as price for the underlying asset Need to do analysis of 4 different types- 1  Long Buildup If the price goes up and Put Open Interest goes up then it is Long buildup.  Short Buildup If the price goes down and Call  Open Interest goes up then it is Short buildup.  Long Unwinding The price will decrease and Put Open interest will also decrease. It is Long unwinding.  Short Covering The price will increase and Call open interest will decrease. This signifies Short covering.   The data will need to be segregated on above basis and analysis needs to be done by examining next day's price whether the prediction (categorization) was proven right  Graphs of 4 categories will be needed.  2 Put Call Ratio analysis:  Put Call Ratio= Total Put OI/Total Call OI  If PCR ratio stays below the previous day closing PCR, then market is likely to be bearish .If the PCR ratio stays above the previous day closing PCR then I market is likely to be bullish.  3 Max Pain  Max Pain Calculation Steps of calculating max pain: Find the difference in strike price and stock price. Find the product of the results and open interest at the strike price. Sum up the rupee value for the put and call at the strike price. For each strike price, repeat the steps. The strike price with the highest value is equivalent to the price of max pain. Check whether on expiry day the underlying price has closed near max pain price.  4 OI based Support & resistance. Find out which CE & PE strike price has the highest OI Build up and check if on expiry day underlying price has crossed these levels.

job_title: gaming cheating detection using response time anomaly in meachine learning
job_description: Detection of cheating in gaming can be a complex problem, but one approach that can be used is the analysis of response time anomalies in machine learning.  The basic idea is to model the normal behavior of a player based on their response times to various events in the game. Then, using machine learning techniques such as clustering or classification, the model can be used to identify unusual patterns in response times that could indicate cheating. For example, if a player's response time is significantly shorter than what would be expected based on their normal behavior, this could be an indication that they are using some form of cheat or automation to gain an advantage.  There are a number of challenges in implementing this approach, including dealing with noise and variability in response times, ensuring that the model is robust against attempts to evade detection, and avoiding false positive detections. However, with the right methods and data, response time anomaly detection can be an effective way to detect cheating in online gaming.

job_title: Listen to Discord messages in R
job_description: I want to develop a simple code in R that listen/waits for new messages from a Discord channel.  Currently, I am running an R code that uses the R function GET to get all the messages in a Discord Channel, then R does an analysis of such messages. This is a time sensitive operation, so I make such a request for new messages every 1 second. As you can image, many of my requests are unnecessary as there may not be new messages.  What I want you to do is a simple code in R that: 1. uses the web-hook from a Discord channel 2. waits for a new message to arrive and then as they arrive 3. does an operation.  The test of the code should be like this: 1. Send a message to the discord channel with the number 2 2. R should be iddle while no messages are transmitted, but as the message arrives, R take the number 2, adds 2, and then print the results. So the R console should show the number 4. 3. If I send a message to the Discord channel with the number 5 , R should print 7.... you get the idea...basically do a small process for each new message. The process is done as the message arrives and not each set time interval. This code needs to be done in R, and the simpler the better. Each line of the code needs to be commented, so i know what you have done.  This is a pretty basic function in Python from the module Discord.py. And there are several R packages (shiny, webhook, webhooks, websocket) that claim to do this, but I failed to implement them as they all require the URL to be wss:// and not https:// as provided by the discord webhook.  Important: The discord console should be closed; in other words, this procedure is to be done remotely: I can run the R code anywhere and get the messages from Discord.  Here is an example of a functional webhook in R, using the wss:// lines. [login to view URL]

However now the number on the tile now also represents the cot of moving that tile (moving the tile marked 6 costs 6). Your program should be called expense_8_puzzle and the command line invocation should follow the following format: [login to view URL] <start-file> <goal-file> <method> <dump-flag> <start-file> and <goal-file> are required. <method> can be bfs - Breadth First Search ucs - Uniform Cost Search dfs - Depth First Search dls - Depth Limited Search (Note: Depth Limit will be obtained as a Console Input) [Note: This part is EC for CSE 4308 students] ids - Iterative Deepening Search [Note: This part is EC for CSE 4308 students] greedy - Greedy Seach a* - A* Search (Note: if no <method> is given, this should be the default option) If <dump-flag>  is given as true, search trace is dumped for analysis in trace-<date>-<time>.txt (Note: if <dump-flag> is not given, assume it is false) search trace contains: fringe and closed set contents per loop of search(and per iteration for IDS), counts of nodes expanded and nodes

job_title: Freelance Data Analysis Project: Analyzing the Relationship between Global Oil Prices, Local Fuel Prices, and Other Local Factors
job_description: We are seeking a freelance data analyst to conduct a comprehensive analysis of the relationship between global oil prices, local fuel prices, and other local factors affecting fuel prices for a fuel station retailer. The project will use data from the past 5 years, including local fuel price data provided by us, and global oil prices such as Brent and crude oil, which the freelancer will obtain from reliable online sources. We will also provide other locally related data such as fuel imports data and etc.  The objective of this project is to identify patterns and trends to help understand the relationship between global oil prices and local fuel prices, as well as other local factors that may affect fuel prices. The freelancer will be responsible for importing and cleaning the data, including identifying and dealing with any missing or erroneous data points. The data will be transformed into a format that can be analyzed, and appropriate statistical and data visualization techniques will be applied to explore the relationship between global oil prices, local fuel prices and other local factors.  The analysis will include examining how changes in global oil prices impact local fuel prices, and how local fuel prices vary based on the volume of oil imports into the country. Additionally, we will explore the impact of other factors such as seasonality, weather temperature, and economic indicators on local fuel prices. By considering all these factors, we aim to provide a comprehensive view of the dynamics of the local fuel market.  The results of the analysis will be presented in the form of interactive dashboards and visualizations, which will allow the fuel station retailer to identify patterns and trends in the data easily. The insights from the analysis can help the retailer make informed decisions about pricing strategies, inventory management, and other business operations related to the sale of fuel.  We are looking for a freelancer who has a strong background in data analysis, statistical modeling, and data visualization. The candidate should be proficient in software tools such as Excel, Power BI, and R or Python. They should have experience in working with large datasets and be able to handle data cleaning and manipulation. The freelancer should be able to deliver the project within the given timeframe and communicate effectively with the client to ensure the project meets their requirements.  Overall, this project provides an exciting opportunity for a freelance data analyst to showcase their skills and deliver valuable insights into the relationship between global oil prices and local fuel prices, as well as other local factors affecting fuel prices.
